speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: Most products are better versions of things that existed before.
zh: 大多数产品是以前存在事物的更好版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about how you get over that hump of copying.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost a moral arbitrage.

zh: 构建消费者社交应用，很少有人成功做到并建立了持久的东西。

en: We have, beyond a latent demand for social, it's lost the adrenaline.
zh: 我们有，除了对社交的潜在需求之外，它已经失去了肾上腺素。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are proud to tell you they're not on Instagram.
zh: 人们很自豪地告诉你他们不在 Instagram 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not missing the party.

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to reinvent social, look for where the cocktail is.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 你能感觉到它，你会想，“噢，我很高兴我在这里。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And over the past five years, he has been working on a book that synthesizes all of the things that he's learned about building successful consumer products.
zh: 在过去的五年里，他一直在写一本书，综合了他学到的关于构建成功消费品的所有东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's called Life at the Speed of Play.
zh: 这本书叫《以游戏的速度生活》(Life at the Speed of Play)。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's coming out in a few weeks and it is so good and it's also a really quick read.
zh: 几周后就要出版了，它非常棒，而且读起来真的很快。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a quote for the book, Sam Altman, the co-founder of OpenAI, said that, "Today, the only bottleneck to building great products is knowing what to create.
zh: 在这本书的一段引用中，OpenAI 的联合创始人 Sam Altman 说，“今天，构建伟大产品的唯一瓶颈是知道该创造什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mark is an expert at this."
zh: Mark 是这方面的专家。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And after reading this book and having this conversation, I could not agree more.
zh: 在读完这本书并进行这次对话后，我非常赞同。

speaker: Unknown
en: In his book and in this conversation, Mark shares a really clever and counterintuitive framework that he's developed for coming up with successful product ideas.
zh: 在他的书中和这次对话中，Mark 分享了他开发的一个非常聪明和反直觉的框架，用于提出成功的产品想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why being less ambitious is often the path to coming up with the most ambitious ideas, why you need to kill your hope before your hope kills you, why your instincts are usually right, but your ideas are usually wrong.
zh: 为什么降低雄心往往是产生最雄心勃勃想法的途径，为什么你需要在希望杀死你之前杀死你的希望，为什么你的直觉通常是对的，但你的想法通常是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, what he's learned about raising kids, he's got five, and so, so, so much more.
zh: 还有，他学到的关于抚养孩子的东西，他有五个孩子，以及很多很多更多的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is for anybody who is building a product or thinking about starting a company.
zh: 这一集适合任何正在构建产品或考虑创办公司的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before we get into it, don't forget to check out lennysproductpass.com for a free year of the hottest and most well-crafted AI products in the world available exclusively to Lenny's Newsletter subscribers.
zh: 在进入正题之前，别忘了去 lennysproductpass.com 免费获得世界上最热门和精心制作的 AI 产品一年的使用权，这专门提供给 Lenny 的 Newsletter 订阅者。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Mark Pincus.

speaker: Unknown
en: Mark, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been a big listener.
zh: 我一直是这档播客的忠实听众。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 噢，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm excited to finally be on.
zh: 我很兴奋终于能上节目了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to finally have you on.
zh: 我很兴奋终于有你来做客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just put out a book.
zh: 你刚出了一本书。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's coming out around the same time that this podcast is coming out.
zh: 它出版的时间和这期播客发布的时间差不多。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's called Life at the Speed of Play.
zh: 这本书叫《以游戏的速度生活》(Life at the Speed of Play)。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much here I want to talk about.
zh: 这里有很多我想讨论的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to start with this framework that you developed called Proven, Better, New.
zh: 我想从你开发的一个框架开始，叫做“已证实的、更好的、新的”(Proven, Better, New)。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the framework is basically designed to help you come up with and refine your product ideas and your startup ideas to basically increase the odds that your idea is a good idea and that it will work.
zh: 这个框架基本上旨在帮助你提出和完善你的产品想法和你的初创想法，基本上增加你的想法是一个好主意并且会成功的几率。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is such a clever idea, it's such a simple idea with so much depth.
zh: 这是一个非常聪明的想法，这是一个非常简单的想法，但有着非常深的深度。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to spend a bunch of time on this.
zh: 我想花很多时间在这个上面。

speaker: Unknown
en: So first of all, just give us kind of the overview on this framework, what it is, how people might use it in coming up with ideas.
zh: 所以首先，给我们这个框架的一个概述，它是什么，人们在提出想法时可能如何使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, this is a framework that we got to early on at Zynga and it became like a religion and the fundamental, the core principles and engine behind how we did product management at Zynga.
zh: 嗯，这是我们在 Zynga 早期采用的一个框架，它变得像一种宗教，成为我们在 Zynga 进行产品管理的核心原则和引擎。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was so fundamental to the book that for three of the four years writing the book, we called it Proven, Better, New.
zh: 它对这本书来说太基础了，以至于在写这本书的四年里，有三年我们把它叫做 Proven, Better, New。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then I was like, "Oh, that's too dry a name."
zh: 但后来我想，“噢，这名字太干巴了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love this concept of life at the speed of play, which we can talk about, but is the bigger gestalt of the book.
zh: 我喜欢“以游戏速度生活”这个概念，我们可以谈谈这个，但这是书中更大的整体概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: But Proven, Better, New is this core philosophy that I've had around products since I started Zynga, which is that we have these instincts in our gut, these human level instincts that are pretty much always right.
zh: 但 Proven, Better, New 是一个核心哲学，自从我创立 Zynga 以来就一直围绕产品持有这种哲学，即我们在直觉中有这些本能，这些人类水平的本能几乎总是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we put these ideas on top of the instincts that are usually wrong.
zh: 然后我们将这些想法置于这些本能之上，而这些想法通常是错误的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my rule of thumb is your instincts are right 95% of the time, your ideas are wrong 75% or at best right 25% of the time.
zh: 我的经验法则是，你的直觉 95% 的时间是正确的，而你的想法 75% 的时间是错误的，或者充其量只有 25% 的时间是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The framework of Proven, Better, New takes that philosophy and says, "Okay, what do we do with that?"
zh: Proven, Better, New 这个框架采用了那个哲学并说道，“好吧，我们对此做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, let's isolate your innovation zone, let's isolate that thing you have in your gut, and let's just test many, many ideas around that and let's fail for the right reason, not the wrong reason."
zh: 好吧，让我们隔离你的创新区域，让我们隔离你直觉中拥有的东西，让我们只是围绕它测试许多许多想法，让我们为了正确的原因失败，而不是错误的原因。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, at Zynga, we'd see new game launches.
zh: 我的意思是，在 Zynga，我们会看到新游戏发布。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sid Meiers, the godfather of game design, who's the most revered game designer, he came out with, I think, a social Civilization on Facebook and we thought, "Oh, God, here comes the ultimate game designer."
zh: Sid Meiers，游戏设计的教父，最受尊敬的游戏设计师，他推出了，我想，Facebook 上的一款社交文明游戏，我们认为，“噢，天哪，终极游戏设计师来了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And 10 minutes after the game came out, the Zynga PMs emailed around their analysis and they said it's dead on arrival because his FTUE, his first time user experience was so many clicks and so bad that no one was ever going to see his great game design.
zh: 在游戏发布 10 分钟后，Zynga 的产品经理们邮件群发了他们的分析，他们说这游戏一出生就死了，因为他的 FTUE，他的首次用户体验，有太多的点击，太糟糕了，以至于没人会看到他出色的游戏设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even Sid Meiers tripped over what were understood by the most junior product managers at Zynga was the best of breed approach to onboarding a new user to the first time user experience on the Facebook platform.
zh: 即使是 Sid Meiers 也在 Zynga 最初级的产品经理所理解的为 Facebook 平台上的首次用户体验引入新用户的最佳方法上跌倒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But because he didn't perfectly copy that, he didn't do the proven right, his innovation never got seen by anybody.
zh: 但因为他没有完美地复制那个，他没有做对 Proven，他的创新从未被任何人看到。

en: So the point of Proven, Better, New is to say, "Let's take all the proven off the table."
zh: 所以 Proven, Better, New 的重点是说，“让我们把所有 Proven 拿出讨论范围。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to build the AI Snapchat or the AI camera, there's so many of those I see now, AI cameras, fine.
zh: 如果你想构建 AI Snapchat 或 AI 相机，我现在看到很多这样的，AI 相机，好的。

en: Let's start with what you're not innovating on, the icon.
zh: 让我们从你不进行创新的地方开始，图标。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe just the way a camera works.
zh: 也许只是相机的运作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Look for where that's the best of breed proven, whether it's Apple or Snapchat or Instagram, and copy those legally.
zh: 寻找哪里是最佳品种的 Proven，无论是 Apple 还是 Snapchat 或 Instagram，并合法地复制它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And with hopefully some taste we can get into great copies and bad copies, but be a master of the proven first.
zh: 并且希望带有一些品味，我们可以区分好的副本和坏的副本，但首先要成为 Proven 的大师。

speaker: Unknown
en: Get your PhD in proven first.
zh: 首先获得你在 Proven 方面的博士学位。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like to say we haven't earned the right to innovate on the camera until we are the world's leading PhD on the best mobile cameras that already exist.
zh: 我喜欢说，在我们成为世界上现有的最佳移动相机的世界领先的博士学位持有者之前，我们还没有权利在相机上进行创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then better is usually we can't find better.
zh: 然后更好通常是我们找不到更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Better is usually very small increments and innovations and better is something that 10 out of 10 of the existing users of that product would say, "Fuck yeah."
zh: 更好通常是非常小的增量和创新，更好是那种该产品的 10 个现有用户中有 10 个会说，“太他妈好了”的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10 out of 10, not you.
zh: 10 分之 10，不是你。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you think is better is called new.
zh: 你认为的更好叫做新。

speaker: Unknown
en: What we think is better is new.
zh: 我们认为的更好是新。

speaker: Unknown
en: We usually are too ambitious on the new and so better could be it's now free, there's no download.
zh: 我们通常在新上面太雄心勃勃了，所以更好可能是它现在是免费的，没有下载。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's something that you can both statistically show works, is better and every user would say yes to.
zh: 有一些东西你可以统计上证明有效，是更好，并且每个用户都会说 yes 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And usually it's very small and it's polished and it's things that intense users, power users will notice the most.
zh: 通常它非常小，经过打磨，那些重度用户，高级用户会最注意到的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in our game, Words With Friends, it was Scrabble, but if it was just Scrabble, why was it a massive hit with 14 million DAUs and Scrabble itself wasn't, right?

speaker: Unknown
en: There must have been something better and novel about it, but it had such a polish for mobile was the better.
zh: 必须有更好和更新的东西，但它对移动端有如此高的打磨度就是更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the new, what's the novel new idea, the back of the box idea that's going to get people to download and try it?
zh: 而新，那个新颖的新想法是什么，那个盒背面的想法，将让人们下载并尝试它？

speaker: Unknown
en: In the case of Words with Friends, it was social.
zh: 就《Words With Friends》而言，它是社交的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was your friends are just ...
zh: 它是你的朋友们只是……

speaker: Unknown
en: It was attached to the Facebook graph and your friends are just already there to play with you, which was a new idea.
zh: 它连接到 Facebook 图谱，你的朋友们已经准备好和你一起玩了，这是一个新想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even that which you'd think, "Of course I want ..." You might not get 10 out of 10 people saying yes, and we have to accept that that new idea is probably going to fail.
zh: 即使那个你会想，“当然我想要……”你可能得不到 10 分之 10 的人说 yes，我们必须接受那个新想法很可能会失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a reason to try it for people, but it's probably going to fail.
zh: 这是人们尝试它的一个理由，但它很可能会失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if we do Proven, Better, New right, the product has much better odds of succeeding in the market, not failing for the wrong reasons.
zh: 如果我们把 Proven、Better、New 做对了，该产品在市场上成功的几率就会大得多，不会因为错误的原因而失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if we start with the premise that the new is probably not right and we've got four other new ideas ready to test, then we just prosecute it in a different way.
zh: 如果我们从「新的很可能是不对的」这一前提开始，并且我们要准备好另外四个新想法来测试，那么我们只是以不同的方式来进行它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to think if you do Proven, Better, New right, it's like a time machine because what if I could go back to Mark 2003 and '4 and say, "Dude, you don't even realize how right you are.
zh: 我喜欢想，如果你把 Proven、Better、New 做对了，这就像一台时光机，因为如果我能回到 2003 年和 2004 年的 Mark 那里，说：「伙计，你甚至没意识到你是对得多么离谱。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of your instincts is going to be a $1.6 trillion company once," and you are a year ahead of them, right?
zh: 你的直觉之一将最终成为一个价值 1.6 万亿美元的公司，」而且你领先他们一年，对吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a year ahead of a $1.6 trillion company.
zh: 你领先一家价值 1.6 万亿美元的公司一年。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not going to tell you which, I'm not going to totally cheat, but I'm just going to tell you, "There's something, there's another answer in this that you could pursue that'd be worth 1.6 trillion.
zh: 我不会告诉你那是哪家，我不打算完全作弊，但我只是想告诉你，「这里有某种东西，这里还有另一个答案你可以追求，那将值 1.6 万亿。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, and by the way, your Tribes idea, that's Reddit, that becomes a whole company industry listings."
zh: 噢，顺便说一句，你的 Tribes 想法，那就是 Reddit，那变成了一个完整的行业列表公司。」

speaker: Unknown
en: So there was a point in Tribe where I knew my metrics weren't working, our D30 retention was terrible.
zh: 所以在 Tribe 的某个时刻，我知道我的指标不起作用，我们的 D30 留存率很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were a sinking speedboat.
zh: 我们是一艘正在下沉的快艇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if I had had this attitude of I'm going to try a lot more ideas, I'm going to look around me.
zh: 如果我当时有这种态度，我要尝试更多的想法，我要环顾四周。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're doing Proven, Better New, you're also looking around you for what are you finding heat that's proven in the market that you can also go test?
zh: 如果你在做 Proven、Better、New，你也在环顾四周，寻找你在市场上发现了什么被证明是火热的东西，你也可以去测试？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I would have massively changed my odds of success.
zh: 那我就会极大地改变我成功的几率。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much here.
zh: 这里有太多东西了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just to summarize how I think about this framework, which is so good, if you start to really think through all the products that have succeeded and how many of them actually follow this, whether they were conscious or not, it's wild.
zh: 所以只是为了总结我对这个框架的看法，它太棒了，如果你开始真正思考所有成功的产品，以及它们中有多少实际上是遵循这个的，无论它们是有意识还是无意识的，这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So kind of the steps that I hear is, so Proven, Better, New.
zh: 所以我听到的步骤是，就是 Proven、Better、New。

speaker: Unknown
en: Proven is make a list of the things that are proven to be working already in the market, things people love about this sort of space.
zh: Proven 是列出已经在市场上被证明有效的东西列表，人们喜欢这个领域的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Better is make something that is not just better, but that 10 out of 10 people will say, "Eff yeah, I would switch.
zh: Better 是制造某种不仅更好，而且 10 个人中有 10 个人会说，「靠，是的，我会切换。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is better."
zh: 这个更好。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And then add something new that nobody's tried before, that adds a little wrinkle.
zh: 然后添加一些以前没人尝试过的新东西，那会增加一点小波折。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think a little bit like you have a bunch of examples in the book, Slack and Threads.
zh: 我认为有点像你在书里有一堆例子，Slack 和 Threads。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's also like, if you think about the iPhone and the iPod, you don't think about them that way, but basically the iPod, there was a music player, they made it better and they added some new stuff and it-
zh: 还有就像，如果你考虑 iPhone 和 iPod，你不会那样想它们，但基本上 iPod，有一个音乐播放器，他们让它变得更好，并添加了一些新东西，它——

speaker: Mark Pincus
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... was one of the best products in the world.
zh: ……是世界上最好的产品之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was there at the TED conference when this team from MIT was demoing their touchscreen.
zh: 当那个来自 MIT 的团队演示他们的触摸屏时，我在 TED 会议现场。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they did it on a gigantic whiteboard and Steve Jobs was obsessed with it.
zh: 他们在一块巨大的白板上做演示，Steve Jobs 痴迷于此。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had a whiteboard and they had a table with it and I watched him and he spent the whole time there with this team obsessing over their touch.
zh: 他们有一块白板，还有一张带它的桌子，我看着他，他在那里花了整个时间与这个团队一起痴迷于他们的触摸。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if that's the first time he saw it, but I know he was obsessed with it like, okay, there's his new idea is a touchscreen.
zh: 我不知道这是不是他第一次看到它，但我知道他对此很痴迷，就像，好的，他的新想法是触摸屏。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's only new idea.
zh: 它是唯一的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to say proven gets so misused.
zh: 我想说 proven 被如此误用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that what I've found over the years with founders is that they start to use Proven, Better, New to justify a wrong idea.
zh: 我多年来在创始人身上发现的是，他们开始使用 Proven、Better、New 来为一个错误的想法辩护。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "Look, Mark, I'm doing proven.
zh: 他们就像，「看，Mark，我在做 proven。

speaker: Unknown
en: Proven is this game that was popular in the '90s.
zh: Proven 是这款 90 年代流行的游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: Proven is," and they pick something and it's to their own detriment, they're not using proven ... We have to be precise.
zh: Proven 是，」然后他们挑选了某种东西，这对他们不利，他们没有使用 proven……我们必须精确。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to think at the pixel level of this experience.
zh: 我们必须在这个体验的像素层面思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: Proven is on this platform for this audience, for this experience.
zh: Proven 是在这个平台上，针对这个受众，针对这种体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can look to what's been proven before as great sources of new ideas, but if it's not on this platform, it doesn't count as proven.
zh: 你可以参考以前被证明的东西作为新想法的极好来源，但如果它不在这个平台上，就不算作 proven。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet the masters at this product craft do Proven, Better, New, whether they call it that or not, and they do it at such a beautiful level that nobody even thinks ... No one realizes what it's derivative of.
zh: 然而这些产品工艺的大师们执行 Proven、Better、New，无论他们是否这样称呼，他们在如此美丽的层面上做到这一点，以至于没有人甚至想……没人意识到它是衍生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Slack was a great example because I think Slack might've just been proven and better and no new and that's even better.
zh: Slack 是一个很好的例子，因为我认为 Slack 可能只是 proven 和 better，没有 new，这甚至更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you can have a successful product, and I don't want to sound anti-innovation, but people don't like change.
zh: 如果你能有一个成功的产品，我不想听起来反创新，但人们不喜欢改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you take a behavior they like, but you make it much more accessible or something much more fun about it, like in the case of Slack, people love that even more.
zh: 所以如果你采用他们喜欢的行为，但你让它更容易获得，或者让它更有趣，就像 Slack 的情况一样，人们更爱它。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this thread of copying that comes through this framework that will turn a lot of people off.
zh: 有一种复制的线索贯穿这个框架，这会让很多人感到反感。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what you think people are missing and how you get over that hump of like this is actually the right approach in most cases.
zh: 谈谈你认为人们错过了什么，以及你如何克服那个障碍，比如这实际上在大多数情况下是正确的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the Peter Thiel sense, it's almost a moral arbitrage because there's something in our gut as a product maker.
zh: 在 Peter Thiel 的意义上，这几乎是一种道德套利，因为作为产品制造者，我们内心深处有某种东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You became a founder, an entrepreneur because you wanted to go be an innovator.
zh: 你成为创始人、创业者是因为你想去成为创新者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it can feel like a beat down that your path to innovation starts with copying other people's work.
zh: 所以这感觉像是一种打击，你通往创新的路径始于复制别人的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we are taught in school, copying is cheating.
zh: 我们在学校里学到，复制就是作弊。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a lot of good reasons why we've built this kind of moral resistance to copying.
zh: 所以有很多很好的理由说明我们建立了这种对复制的道德抵制。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that also makes that opportunity in some ways more available for people who have less ego involved.
zh: 但这也让那个机会在某种程度上对那些自我意识较少的人更容易获得。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like to say, and I said this to my product makers at Zynga, if you're truly ambitious, burn your resume.
zh: 我喜欢说，我也对 Zynga 的产品制造者说过，如果你真的有野心，烧掉你的简历。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you define your ambition in the eyes of your consumer, not your peers, you're not trying to win awards and respect from your peers, you're trying to win the hearts and minds of nurses in Indiana, like for Farmville, you're going to define innovation differently and you're not going to worry about whether ... You're going to take the best ideas wherever you can find them if they are in service of giving her an experience that she loves more.
zh: 如果你在消费者的眼中定义你的雄心，而不是你的同龄人，你不是试图赢得奖项和同龄人的尊重，你是试图赢得印第安纳州护士的心和思想，就像对于 Farmville 一样，你会以不同的方式定义创新，而且你不会去担心是否……只要能为她带来她更爱的体验，你就会随处寻找最好的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if all you did was copy, there's no reason for her to choose your product, right?
zh: 所以如果你所做的只是复制，她没有理由选择你的产品，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you took something that she loves and you make it one inch better, she might love that more than if you showed her something she's never seen before and didn't wake up knowing that she wanted.
zh: 但如果你拿走了她喜欢的东西，并且让它稍微好一点点，她可能会更喜欢它，而不是你向她展示她从未见过的东西、而她醒来时并不知道她想要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that the art of this is you do it in a way that she doesn't even realize it.
zh: 我认为其中的艺术在于，你这样做的方式甚至让她没有意识到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I think consumer taste also resists just a copy.
zh: 因为我认为消费者的品味也抵制单纯的复制。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that if movies, TV shows, books, if they feel too derivative and they're not adding something important and new, then it could go against our consumer taste.
zh: 我认为如果电影、电视节目、书籍，如果它们感觉太衍生了，而且没有添加重要和新颖的东西，那么它可能会违背我们的消费者品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the best product makers, and I love referencing someone like Craig Newmark and Craigslist, who took two years to add photos to Craigslist listings.
zh: 但最好的产品制造者，我喜欢引用像 Craigslist 这样的人，他花了两年时间才在 Craigslist 列表中添加照片。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he lived in my neighborhood and he was friends with my dog, Zynga, more than he was friends with me.
zh: 他住在我家附近，他和我的狗 Zynga 的交情比和我的交情还深。

speaker: Unknown
en: He was a little awkward socially and he connected more with the dog and I tried to be friends with him through the dog.
zh: 他在社交上有点尴尬，他和狗的连接更多，我试图通过狗和他交朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he would sit and talk to me and he was working on ...
zh: 所以他会坐下来跟我说话，他在从事……

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "What are you working on?"
zh: 我就说，“你在做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He said, "I've been working for two years on adding photos to listings."
zh: 他说，“我花了两年时间在列表中添加照片。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "What do you mean two years?"
zh: 我就说，“你说两年是什么意思？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Well, I really want to make sure that people like it and that the photos come up in the right ways."
zh: 他就说，“嗯，我真的想确保人们喜欢它，而且照片以正确的方式出现。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I honestly don't even understand what he did for two years.
zh: 我真的甚至都不明白他做了两年什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're driving across San Francisco to buy someone's couch, why isn't that better?
zh: 但如果你开车穿越旧金山去买某人的沙发，为什么那不是更好的呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: In his mind, it was new.
zh: 在他的脑海里，那是新的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "That's a world-class product maker because what he gets is that his ..."
zh: 我就想，“那是一个世界级的产品制造者，因为他明白的是他的……”

speaker: Unknown
en: We feel this sense of ownership of products that we rely on every day and we're angry when they change.
zh: 我们对我们每天依赖的产品有一种主人翁感，当它们改变时我们会很生气。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if they change for the better, we're angry because we go through life faster because of pattern recognition and we don't want to think about these things.
zh: 即使它们变得更好了，我们也会生气，因为我们通过模式识别在生活中过得更快，我们不想考虑这些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what if by adding photos to his listings, he moved where the text was and what if you rifle through listings and now the text is below the fold?
zh: 如果通过在他的列表中添加照片，他移动了文本的位置，如果你翻阅列表，现在文本在折叠之下，会怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: And a junior product maker might make the whole thing a picture and not realize that what people liked most was being able to see the text real quick and they were trying to compare prices on the couch they already knew that ...
zh: 一个初级的产品制造者可能会把整个东西做成图片，而没有意识到人们最喜欢的是能够很快地看到文本，并且他们试图比较沙发的价格，他们已经知道……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you're talking, I'm just thinking what are all the best products and are they all just kind of one word ... Are they copies and evolutions of existing products or how many are just completely brand new?
zh: 当你在说话时，我就在想所有最好的产品是什么，它们是否都只是一个词……它们是现有产品的复制和演变，还是有多少是全新的？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm looking around like the iPhone, there's phones before and they kind of took the elements of what was great, made them awesome.
zh: 我环顾四周，就像 iPhone，之前有手机，它们基本上采用了伟大的元素，把它们变得很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: My Owala, I don't know, water bottle, there was water bottles that worked and then they added things to make it better.
zh: 我的 Owala，我不知道，水瓶，之前有能用的水瓶，然后它们添加了东西让它变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Chrome, I don't know.
zh: Chrome，我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just looking around.
zh: 我只是在环顾四周。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting how once you start to think about it through this lens, most products are better versions of things that existed before.
zh: 一旦你开始通过这个视角思考，大多数产品是以前存在的东西的更好版本，这太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: A.
zh: A。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nd then I'm thinking about as a product team, what do you often do?
zh: 然后我作为一个产品团队在思考，你经常做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You go, look at the competitors, look at all their flows, here's what they're doing, here's what's working, look at all these great ideas, and then you build on that.
zh: 你会去看竞争对手，看他们所有的流程，他们在做什么，什么有效，看所有这些伟大的想法，然后你在其基础上构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's easy to get turned off to this idea of the word copy, but as you think about it, this is mostly how products end up getting built anyway.
zh: 我认为很容易对复制这个词的想法感到反感，但当你思考它时，这基本上也就是产品最终被构建的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, there's so much on this that we could literally spend the whole podcast on it, which I'd be fine with too, or do a whole episode just on this because so much comes up for me.
zh: 嗯，关于这一点有太多内容了，我们真的可以在整个播客中讨论它，我也完全没问题，或者专门做一集来讲这个，因为我想到了太多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the one hand, I think about Nikita, who I think you've had on.
zh: 一方面，我想到了 Nikita，我认为你请过他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love his story on TBH.
zh: 我喜欢他关于 TBH 的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a perfect example of he found something proven in someone else's product.
zh: 这是一个完美的例子，他在别人的产品中发现了一些被证明过的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was my first company, Freeloader.
zh: 那是我的第一家创业公司，Freeloader。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I found was in the Netscape browser and in the Internet Explorer browser, they had buried in there a feature of offline browsing.
zh: 我发现的是在 Netscape 浏览器和 Internet Explorer 浏览器中，它们在里面隐藏了一个离线浏览的功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my entire product was offline browsing.
zh: 而我的整个产品就是离线浏览。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they had it as this buried power user feature and I saw the genius in that for that moment because bandwidth was so slow that I was like, "Oh, we can make an entire product just around that one feature."
zh: 但他们把它作为一个隐藏的资深用户功能，我看到了那一刻其中的天才，因为带宽太慢了，我就想，“噢，我们可以只围绕那一个功能做一个完整的产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Nikita saw his perfect product buried inside an Arabic only version of his product and he said, "Oh my God, they nailed it."
zh: Nikita 看到了他的完美产品隐藏在他产品的仅阿拉伯语版本中，他说，“天哪，他们做到了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think when we can see something that is proven already in someone else's product, but they have the wrong idea around it, that's gold.
zh: 所以我认为当我们能看到已经在别人的产品中被证明过的东西，但他们围绕它的想法是错误的，那就是金子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then to your question of is every product derivative?
zh: 然后关于你的每个产品都是衍生的这个问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is every product from Proven, Better, New?
zh: 每个产品都来自 Proven、Better、New 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: My answer is no.
zh: 我的回答是不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I talked about this a little bit in the book and in my course, but the question is at the outset for all of us, which path do you want to be on?
zh: 我在书中和课程中稍微谈到了这一点，但问题在于一开始对于我们所有人，你想走哪条路？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I argue that if you go down this path of just innovate, start with a blank whiteboard, don't look at any other products, that is kind of Rovio getting to Angry Birds.
zh: 我认为如果你走上这条路，只是创新，从空白白板开始，不看任何其他产品，那有点像 Rovio 走向 Angry Birds。

speaker: Unknown
en: They made 45 games.
zh: 他们做了 45 款游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every single game was totally different, not, as far as I could tell, learning from the previous failures or the market and their 45th shot on goal was Angry Birds and it was a hit and it was innovative.
zh: 每一款游戏都完全不同，据我所知，没有从之前的失败或市场中吸取教训，他们的第 45 次射门是《愤怒的小鸟》，它一炮而红，而且很有创新性。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the odds, that's wildcat drilling and do you want to have those odds?
zh: 但是这种几率，那是野猫式钻探，你想要这种几率吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you want to do what OMGPOP did, which is very similar story but opposite path, which was OMGPOP created the hit game Draw Something and Zynga bought them.
zh: 还是你想做 OMGPOP 那样的事，故事非常相似但路径相反，OMGPOP 创造了热门游戏《你画我猜》，然后 Zynga 收购了他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were the number one game and app in the App Store for 60 days in 2012.
zh: 2012 年，他们曾是 App Store 上排名第一的游戏和应用，为期 60 天。

speaker: Unknown
en: But OMGPOP was on their last dollar.
zh: 但 OMGPOP 当时已经到了穷途末路。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had tried something totally innovative.
zh: 他们尝试了完全创新的东西。 [[207] ]以前没人见过或尝试过，但彻底失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one ever seen or tried it before, it failed completely.
zh: 以前从来没人见过或尝试过它，它彻底失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now they were ruthlessly, desperately in need of a hit and now they did Proven, Better, New perfectly.
zh: 现在他们无情地、迫切地需要一款热门游戏，现在他们完美地执行了「经过验证的、更好的、新的」。

speaker: Unknown
en: They said, "We're going to take this proven game and do everything about it as perfect students of what is already working and viral on mobile."
zh: 他们说，“我们要采用这款经过验证的游戏，作为已经在移动端有效且病毒式传播的东西的完美学生，把关于它的一切都做好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they perfectly copied our turn-based system from Words With Friends and they made a massive hit.
zh: 他们完美地复制了我们来自《Words With Friends》的回合制系统，然后他们制造了一个巨大的热门。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hell yeah.
zh: 是啊，太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this idea of moral arbitrage, that's such a ...
zh: 我喜欢“道德套利”这个想法，那真是太……

speaker: Unknown
en: Thinking about this-
zh: 想想看这个——

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very Peter Thiel-ian, you know?
zh: 这非常有 Peter Thiel 风格，你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's so many fun threads that kind of connect to all this.
zh: 所以有很多有趣的线索把这些都联系起来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have all these amazing contrarian perspectives on how to build amazing products.
zh: 你有这么多惊人的逆向视角，关于如何构建令人惊叹的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is be less ambitious.
zh: 一个是少一点雄心壮志。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about why that's a really good lens to actually build more ambitious products.
zh: 谈谈为什么这真的是一个很好的视角，去实际构建更有雄心的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm in the middle of relearning that lesson myself right now.
zh: 我现在正在自己重新学习这一课的过程中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I would say there's so many paradoxes in this and one that I've had to learn over and over again is if we're too ambitious at the outset and too ambitious and visionary about the product we want to build, then we will probably miss product market fit because we won't start at a small enough, humble enough place because it might ... Usually the starting point for these products is embarrassingly small.
zh: 我想说这里有很多悖论，我必须一遍又一遍地学习的一个是，如果我们在一开始就太有雄心，对我们想要构建的产品太有远见，那么我们很可能会错过产品市场契合度，因为我们不会从一个足够小、足够卑微的地方开始，因为这可能…… 通常这些产品的起点都尴尬地小。

speaker: Unknown
en: So many of these massive hit products or franchises started with such humble-
zh: 许多这种巨大的热门产品或特许经营权都始于如此卑微的——

speaker: Unknown
en: Started with such humble non-ambitious places and The Facebook was just an app to check out girls and guys at Harvard.
zh: 始于如此卑微的、没有雄心的地方，而且 The Facebook 最初只是一个用来查看哈佛女生和男生的应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the irony for me is I've found that my career has gone in these waves and maybe some of your other founders have had this experience too, that I've started in a very humble place of, "I just want to get to anything, any product market fit", had bigger success than I imagined I would have in my first two companies, Freeloader and Support.com.
zh: 对我来说讽刺的是，我发现我的职业生涯就是这样波浪式前进的，也许你们其他一些创始人也有过这种经历，我从一个非常卑微的地方开始，“我只想达到任何东西，任何产品市场契合度”，比我前两家公司 Freeloader 和 Support.com 想象的更大的成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: They both started with very small, humble premise.
zh: 它们都是从非常小、卑微的前提开始的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then afterwards felt like now I can do anything.
zh: 然后之后觉得现在我可以做任何事了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know Elon is the exception to the rule.
zh: 我知道 Elon 是那个规则的例外。

speaker: Unknown
en: Part of it is I think he can raise magical unlimited amounts of capital, which helps, but then he does not start in humble places at all.
zh: 部分原因是我认为他能筹集到神奇的无限量的资金，这有帮助，但他根本没有从卑微的地方开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: But for the rest of us mortals, I'd say that we tend to after success feel the need to do something bigger the next time.
zh: 但对于我们其他凡人来说，我想说我们倾向于在成功后觉得下次需要做更大的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's human instinct and we want to be more ambitious.
zh: 这是人类的本能，我们想要更有雄心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when I got to Tribe and I saw social networking and LinkedIn was just starting and I saw how big the opportunity was, I tried to do everything with Tribe.
zh: 所以当我到了 Tribe，我看到了社交网络，LinkedIn 刚刚起步，我看到了机会有多大，我试图用 Tribe 做所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was really ambitious and I didn't pick one use case.
zh: 这真的很有雄心，我没有选择一个用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there was multiple inside Tribe that worked.
zh: 而且 Tribe 里面有多个东西是奏效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The tribes, the idea of these urban tribes worked.
zh: 那些部落，这些城市部落的想法是奏效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: People loved it, but it was too ambitious and so we failed.
zh: 人们很喜欢，但它太有雄心了，所以我们失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I was so humbled and I was in this abyss we can talk about for so long and I was so just desperate to get out of that abyss that by the time I got to Zynga, I did something that was embarrassingly small.
zh: 然后我感到非常谦卑，我在这个深渊里我们可以谈论很长时间，我如此迫切地想走出那个深渊，以至于当我到了 Zynga 时，我做了一件非常微小的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, to be 41, multi-time successful founder that could go do something important in the world and what did I do?
zh: 我是说，作为一个 41 岁、多次成功的创始人，可以在世界上做些重要的事情，而我做了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I made a Facebook app like a poker game, not even a Facebook app, I mean a poker game.
zh: 我做了一个像扑克游戏这样的 Facebook 应用，甚至不是一个 Facebook 应用，我是说一个扑克游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was people thought I had no dignity.
zh: 人们觉得我没有尊严。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean people were like, "Mark, there's so much you could do in the world."
zh: 人们说，“Mark，你能在世界上做这么多事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'd say my ambition came down to a 1000 ft altitude, not a 100,000 ft. And that was the key to that being successful.
zh: 但我想说我的雄心降到了 1000 英尺的高度，而不是 10 万英尺。那是它成功的关键。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I see this with, it's just so hard.
zh: 我在许多产品创始人身上看到了这一点，这几乎给了新创始人相对于多次成功创始人的优势，因为我们有太多的绳子来勒死自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see it with so many product founders and it almost gives a new founder an advantage over a multi-time successful founder because we have too much rope to hang ourselves.
zh: 在达到产品市场契合度之前，针对一个大的愿景筹集资金和组建团队太容易了，但世界不在乎你的简历。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's too easy to raise money and recruit teams against a big vision before we've gotten to product market fit, but the world doesn't care about your resume.
zh: 这对观看的每个人来说都是好消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the good news for everyone watching.
zh: 每个人都有相同的机会，在很多方面，如果你处于一个更卑微的地方，你有更好的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone has the same chance and in a lot of ways if you're in a more humble place, you have a better opportunity.
zh: 所以悖论是，你越有雄心，你就应该越卑微，你应该愿意从更小的地方开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the paradox is the more ambitious you are that the more humble you should be in the smaller place you should be willing to start.
zh: 你在这方面的一个见解是，这是你作为初创公司的优势，就是少一点雄心，因为像 Zuck 这样的人需要做得非常大，因为收入已经很高了。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of your insights along these lines is that this is your advantage as a startup is to be less ambitious because a Zuck needs to go really big because the revenue is already so high.
zh: 他们承担的任何事情都需要是数十亿美元的商业机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything they take on needs to be many billion-dollar business opportunity.
zh: 他们承担的任何事项都需要是数十亿美元的商业机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a startup, you don't need to do that.
zh: 作为一家初创公司，你不需要那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is how you end up discovering some of the biggest ideas in the end.
zh: 这就是你最终如何发现一些最大点子的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Being willing to pursue these little threads when they're flaky, when they're not a business, when they're so much earlier, and so many of the successful companies today started there.
zh: 愿意在这些细小线索还很脆弱、不成气候、处于极早期的时候去追求它们，如今许多成功的公司都是从那里起步的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there an example that comes to mind when, because what's a company or two that started very unambitious?
zh: 有没有想到什么例子，因为有哪些一两家公司起步时非常没有野心？

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the story of Bolt.new that really blew up last year.
zh: 我喜欢 Bolt.new 的故事，它去年真的爆火了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I loved it so much.
zh: 我非常喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I coldmailed the founder because I was just so impressed with-
zh: 我冷邮件给创始人，因为我只是对……印象深刻

speaker: Lenny
en: [inaudible 00:29:14]?

speaker: Unknown
en: ... Yeah.
zh: ……是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they toiled an obscurity on something that they were into and then they open sourced it.
zh: 他们在默默无闻中辛苦耕耘着他们感兴趣的东西，然后他们把它开源了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think they were barely able to keep going with commercial development.
zh: 我想他们几乎难以维持商业开发。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they had this realization one day of, "Wait a second, if we take these web stacks, this virtual machine that we've been making work on the web, but we actually add that to an AI coding copilot, we now have something that's better than what anybody else has."
zh: 然后有一天他们意识到，“等一下，如果我们拿这些 Web 技术栈，这个我们一直使其在 Web 上运行的虚拟机，但我们实际上把它加到一个 AI 编程副驾驶中，我们现在就有了比别人都好的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that was just a great story that they were passionate about one thing and they stuck with it.
zh: 我认为这只是一个很棒的故事，他们对一件事充满热情并且坚持了下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Eric was on the podcast.
zh: Eric 上过这个播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talked about that.
zh: 我们谈论过那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, he was?
zh: 噢，是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm so impressed.
zh: 我太佩服了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a lot of ways, Slack also was humbled.
zh: 在很多方面，Slack 也很谦卑。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stewart keeps trying to start game companies and those turn into unexpected bigger hit companies.
zh: Stewart 一直试图创办游戏公司，结果变成了意想不到的更大的爆款公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll see what he does.
zh: 我们看他接下来会做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually now has probably so much capital he could just keep doing a game company this time.
zh: 而且实际上现在可能拥有这么多资本，他这次可以继续做一家游戏公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think to your point, he might try again to unhumble, go big.
zh: 但我认为说到你的观点，他可能会再次尝试不谦卑，以此做大。

speaker: Unknown
en: But each time, so he started a big idea for the mass-market MMO, which I've also loved, but really difficult.
zh: 但每一次，所以他从大众市场 MMO 这个大想法开始，我也很喜欢，但真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they were humbled in that enough to say, "Okay, wait, there's this little teeny thing that our engineers use.
zh: 他们在那方面足够谦卑，以至于说，“好的，等等，有这个小东西，我们的工程师在使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's build a product around that."
zh: 让我们围绕它构建一个产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it really takes a really attuned, curious, humble founder to call the ball on that.
zh: 这真的需要一个非常敏锐、好奇、谦卑的创始人来做出那个决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I can imagine, I guess I've been there how hard it is when you've got investors and team and everybody going in one direction and we have to express confidence as founders.
zh: 我的意思是，我可以想象，我想我有过这种经历，当你有投资人和团队并且每个人都朝一个方向前进时，这有多难，而且我们必须作为创始人表达信心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether or not we have actual confidence inside, that's part of, I think, the dissonance of this.
zh: 无论我们内心是否真的有信心，我认为这都是这种不协调的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you express confidence?
zh: 你如何表达信心？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you stay in a place of intellectual honesty?
zh: 你如何保持一种智力诚实？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we stay authentic and transparent and not rattle our team?
zh: 我们如何保持真实和透明，而不动摇我们的团队？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm still learning.
zh: 我还在学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: I err on the side of transparency and honesty and being authentic to a fault and I burn out people in that process and I did at Zynga and I have since because people will say, the negative critique I'm working with me is, "Oh, working for Mark is like third grade soccer and every Monday he comes in excited about a different idea."
zh: 我倾向于透明、诚实和过度真实，在那过程中我让人筋疲力尽，我在 Zynga 是这样，以后也是因为人们会说，对我的负面批评是，“噢，为 Mark 工作就像三年级足球，每周一他带着一个不同的想法兴奋地进来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're not wrong.
zh: 他们没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: But where I can get better at this maybe is if we can build a culture with our team that says, "Okay, we are ambitious.
zh: 但我可以在这方面做得更好的地方也许是，如果我们能与团队建立一种文化，说，“好的，我们有野心。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're so ambitious that we're not going to hold onto a B+.
zh: 我们非常有野心，我们不会坚守一个 B+ 的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if we could get funding, even if it has some traction, we have a north star that we are not going to stop until we find product market fit against our north star and this isn't it and it's going to take courage on our part.
zh: 即使我们可以获得资金，即使它有一些吸引力，我们有一个北极星指标，在找到符合我们北极星的产品市场契合度之前我们不会停止，而这不是它，这需要我们的勇气。

speaker: Unknown
en: And hey, let's look around the room.
zh: 嘿，让我们看看房间里的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is everyone ambitious enough to do this?
zh: 每个人都有足够的野心这样做吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you want to peel off and go join someone else who's already a rocket ship?"
zh: 还是你们想离开去加入其他人，那家公司已经是一艘火箭了？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a fair career choice for you to make.
zh: 对你来说，这是一个公平的职业选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think that's to me the real founder mode is can you have the courage to tell your team and your investors that this isn't it?
zh: 但我认为对我来说，真正的创始人模式是你是否有勇气告诉你的团队和你的投资者这不是它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's exactly where I wanted to go, which is this other piece of advice you give, which is, "Kill hope before hope kills you."
zh: 这正是我想去的地方，也就是你给出的另一个建议，也就是，“在希望扼杀你之前扼杀希望。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just what that is because so much of your advice is, "Kill your bad ideas quickly."
zh: 谈谈那是什么，因为你的很多建议是，“快速扼杀你的坏主意。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So just talk about that and then I'm really curious to know what it takes to realize an idea is bad.
zh: 所以谈谈那个，然后我真的很好奇知道意识到一个主意是坏的需要什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: When do you decide, "Okay, this is B+.
zh: 你什么时候决定，“好的，这是 B+。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's move on."
zh: 让我们继续前进。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if I get credit for making up that quote, but I love it, kill hope before hope kills you.
zh: 我不知道我是否因为想出了这句话而受到赞誉，但我喜欢它，在希望扼杀你之前扼杀希望。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a difference between belief and hope.
zh: 信念和希望之间有区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hope is confidence without basis.
zh: 希望是没有基础的信心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hope is just, it's a prayer, but it's not founded in anything that your lived experience.
zh: 希望只是一种祈祷，但它并不建立在你的任何生活经验之上。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not founded in your experience with your product, your experience with people experiencing your product and the data.
zh: 它不是建立在你对产品的经验，人们对产品的经验以及数据之上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that too many founders and teams keep going with the hope that this next release is going to do something magical.
zh: 我认为太多的创始人和团队继续抱着希望，觉得下一个版本会带来奇迹。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that the best product makers, I like to say they're collecting winnings, they're not making bets.
zh: 我认为最好的产品制造者，我喜欢说他们是在收集赢利，而不是在下注。

speaker: Unknown
en: They already know.
zh: 他们已经知道了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you talk to Brian Chesky about his launches, he already knows that he has a hit.
zh: 如果你和 Brian Chesky 谈他的发布，他已经知道他会有一款热门产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's not launching it to find out if people like it.
zh: 他发布它不是为了发现人们是否喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I talk about the difference between launching an MVP, a minimum viable product, and a maximum launchable product.
zh: 我谈到发布 MVP（最小可行性产品）和最大可发布产品之间的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if we're going off hope, we're getting to this MVP, minimum viable, that the viable is the word we got to kill along with hope because viable is where hope comes from.
zh: 因为如果我们基于希望，我们就会达到这个 MVP，最小可行，这个“可行”是我们要和希望一起消灭的词，因为可行是希望产生的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: If it's viable, it might make it.
zh: 如果它是可行的，它可能会成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a difference between get in the market early, learn.
zh: 在尽早进入市场、学习之间是有区别的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're putting a product out that you're going to learn from and you're putting a product out that you're going to launch that you think you believe, not hope, is going to be a hit.
zh: 你发布一个产品是为了从中学习，你发布一个产品是为了发布，你认为你相信，而不是希望，它会成为热门。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's really important to be crisp and to make that distinction.
zh: 做到清晰并做出这种区别非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And AI is a dangerous tool today.
zh: AI 如今是一个危险的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so powerful that it's dangerous.
zh: 它太强大了，以至于它很危险。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so powerful that it means we can get to a viable product in much less time and money than it took maybe three months instead of three years.
zh: 它太强大了，意味着我们可以用更少的时间和金钱达到一个可行的产品，可能三个月而不是三年。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a dangerous drug because it means that... What I thought would happen where we'd be today is that people would be using AI to build these incredible testing machines, failure machines that are testing, I like to say more ideas in a week than your industry tests in a year.
zh: 这是一种危险的药物，因为这意味着……我原以为会发生我们在今天的情况是，人们会使用 AI 来构建这些令人难以置信的测试机器，失败机器，这些测试，我喜欢说在一周内测试的想法比你的行业在一年内测试的还要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: How are you testing a 100 ideas a day instead of one in three months?
zh: 你如何每天测试 100 个想法，而不是三个月测试一个？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think AI is being used more to build one idea in three months than a hundred ideas in a day.
zh: 我认为 AI 更多被用来在三个月内构建一个想法，而不是在一天内构建一百个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what would it look like?
zh: 那会是什么样子？

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to say we should build it completely wrong before we know it's the right product.
zh: 我喜欢说我们应该在知道它是正确的产品之前完全错误地构建它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So build it wrong before you know it's right.
zh: 所以在知道它是对的之前先错误地构建它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you could build it right, great, but don't be slowed down by that.
zh: 如果你能正确地构建它，那很好，但不要因此而放慢速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if we start off, if we say, "Okay, what can I believe today?
zh: 因为如果我们开始，如果我们说，“好的，今天我可以相信什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe this is the wrong product."
zh: 我相信这是错误的产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you going to do differently because you believe it's the wrong product?
zh: 因为我相信它是错误的产品，你会做些什么不同的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: You are not going to waste three months building the wrong product.
zh: 你不会浪费三个月去构建错误的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Oh, I'd rather waste a day or a week building the wrong product.
zh: “哦，我宁愿浪费一天或一周去构建错误的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the point of what I'm building is to learn.
zh: 但我构建的意义在于学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: So is it good enough to get signal and to test my idea?"
zh: 那么它是否足以获得信号并测试我的想法？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we can usually pull that apart and test those pieces in the wrong way.
zh: 我认为我们通常可以把它拆开并以错误的方式测试这些部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so often it could be an ad.
zh: 它通常可以是一个广告。

speaker: Unknown
en: I cannot believe how many times a team hasn't even tested the ads for their product before they go and put in the markets an afterthought.
zh: 我无法相信有多少次团队甚至没有为他们产品的广告做测试，就把它们作为事后的想法放入市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was so often at Zynga, it blew my mind.
zh: 而且在 Zynga 经常如此，这让我大吃一惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I'll give you one story from Zynga.
zh: 我的意思是，我会给你讲一个 Zynga 的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we were launching our first expansion pack for FarmVille, which these became massive business drivers for us, I think it was FarmVille English Countryside or something.
zh: 当我们要发布 FarmVille 的第一个扩展包时，这些后来成为了我们巨大的商业驱动力，我认为是 FarmVille English Countryside 或其他什么东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The team came to me and they said, "Okay, we have a $10 million ad budget and we're going to start running ads before the launch."
zh: 团队来找我，他们说，“好的，我们有 1000 万美元的广告预算，我们将在发布前开始投放广告。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Wait, you have 25, 30 million people a day using your product, but you're going to put ads somewhere else for coming soon.
zh: 我说，“等等，你每天有 2500 万、3000 万人使用你的产品，但你要在其他地方投放广告作为即将推出。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have these people engaged.
zh: 你有这些人参与。

speaker: Unknown
en: What if you just put something on the game board?
zh: 如果你只在游戏板上放一些东西会怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: And what if you don't just start your product marketing, you're still doing product testing to see what gets the most heat?
zh: 如果你不仅仅开始你的产品营销，你仍然在做产品测试，看看什么能获得最多的热度会怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: What if you start putting different art forms of your English Countryside locked on the game board and you just see how many clicks you get and you just start testing the look, the feel, the marketing message and at the same time when people click on it says, 'Coming soon, click here to be a pre-user, click here to get access two weeks before anybody else.'"
zh: 如果你在游戏板上锁定你的 English Countryside 的不同艺术形式，你只是看看你获得了多少点击，你只是开始测试外观、感觉、营销信息，同时当人们点击它时，它说‘即将推出，点击这里成为预用户，点击这里比其他任何人都早两周获得权限’会怎么样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it turned out everybody clicked on it because it's a change in your game board and it both gave us direction on what was the right marketing and the right variant of the product, but the unintended consequences was we started selling keys to people for you and a friend and we ended up selling $19 million worth of keys to get early access to the new expansion pack.
zh: 结果每个人都点击了它，因为它是你的游戏板的变化，它既给了我们正确的营销和产品的正确变体的方向，但意外的是我们开始向人们出售钥匙给你和一个朋友，我们最终卖出了价值 1900 万美元的钥匙，以获得新扩展包的早期访问权限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we took something that was going to be afterthought advertising, try to drum up interest in this product and turn into something that gave us signal and direction on the product and revenues.
zh: 所以我们将某种原本是事后营销广告、试图引起人们对这款产品的兴趣的东西，变成了某种在产品上给我们提供信号和方向以及收入的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so often you can have, this is an existing product, but you could turn something that was going to be marketing into a kind of scarcity and heat-driving feature, something that could drive revenues or something else that matters.
zh: 通常你可以拥有，这是一个现有的产品，但你可以将某种原本是营销的东西变成一种稀缺性和热度驱动的功能，某种可以产生收入或其他重要东西的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the way we should be using AI is as a testing machine, a failure machine and a way to vibe code, Quadcode, but build the lowest possible cycled version of your product that you can get signal back on.
zh: 所以我们应该使用 AI 的方式是作为一台测试机器，一台失败机器，以及一种 vibe code，Quadcode，但是构建你能获得信号反馈的最低可能的循环版本的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to ask about something that I think might be on people's minds as they hear you talk.
zh: 我想问一些我认为人们听你谈话时可能会想到的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: For a lot of product builders, when I think Zynga, they're not like, "This is the product I want to build."
zh: 对于许多产品构建者来说，当我想到 Zynga 时，他们并不认为，“这是我想构建的产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a spectrum of products you guys have built.
zh: 你们构建的产品有一个范围。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's like FarmVille and CityVille and all these things that I think turn some people off.
zh: 这就像 FarmVille 和 CityVille 以及所有这些我认为让某些人反感的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's like Poker, Words with Friends, Mafia Wars that I think people are like, "Oh, that's amazing.
zh: 然后有像 Poker、Words with Friends、Mafia Wars 这些，人们就像，"噢，那很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that."
zh: 我喜欢那个。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Can you just speak to that?
zh: 你能谈谈这个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if I'm unpacking that when you say that the products turn people off, it's funny you say that because FarmVille and CityVille were our biggest hits in terms of installs, engagement, retention, revenues.
zh: 我想如果我要拆解这一点，当你说这些产品让人反感时，你这么说很有趣，因为 FarmVille 和 CityVille 是我们在安装量、参与度、留存率和营收方面最大的热门。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, Words with Friends and Poker were more enduring because they made the transition to mobile and the other games didn't.
zh: 现在，Words with Friends 和 Poker 更加持久，因为它们转型到了移动端，而其他游戏没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a whole other story, but I think the part you're getting to is that Zynga was in a lot of ways a victim of its own success, that our games were so viral and took over the feeds on Facebook so much that your friend who was playing it started to bug you as a person not playing it and that's fair.
zh: 这完全是另一个故事，但我认为你所指的部分是 Zynga 在很大程度上成为了自身成功的受害者，我们的游戏是如此具有病毒性，如此占据了 Facebook 的信息流，以至于玩它的朋友开始作为一个不玩它的人来烦你，这是公平的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what I would say, I think what's misunderstood about Zynga from the beginning and why we succeeded and we created eight massive hits out of 10 mass large game launches.
zh: 但我想说的是，我认为从一开始人们对 Zynga 就有误解，以及为什么我们成功，我们在 10 次大型游戏发布中创造了 8 个巨大的热门。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had a very high hit rate.
zh: 我们有很高的命中率。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't that we were good at virality.
zh: 这并不是因为我们擅长病毒式传播。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was that we were focused on two things that I think we did better than anybody else and we had lots of competitors, lots and copying and we still managed to keep winning.
zh: 而是因为我们专注于两件事，我认为我们比其他任何人都做得更好，我们有很多竞争对手，很多抄袭，我们仍然设法保持赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it couldn't be that we were just spammier or better at virality.
zh: 所以不可能只是因为我们更垃圾或更擅长病毒式传播。

speaker: Unknown
en: First, we had this core mission and focus around connecting the world through games and Bing Gordon and I kept coming back to this idea that I love and I want your founders, product makers watching this to join me in thinking about, which is how can we add more dimensionality?
zh: 首先，我们有这个核心使命和重心，即通过游戏连接世界，Bing Gordon 和我一直回到这个我喜欢的理念，我希望观看这个视频的创始人和产品制作者能加入我一起来思考，那就是我们要如何增加更多的维度？

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this idea of, it's why I call it bold beat.
zh: 我喜欢这个理念，这也是我称之为 bold beat 的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's how do I take this thing that's kind of gotten boring and wrote, poker or whatever, how do I add a whole new dimension to it?
zh: 就是我该如何把这个变得有点无聊和死板的东西，扑克或其他什么的，给它增加一个全新的维度？

speaker: Unknown
en: And by adding social, by adding real identities, by giving you this, you're doing social networking, you're in this cocktail party and now we're giving you a way to actually meet new people or improve your relationships in a game.
zh: 通过添加社交功能，通过添加真实身份，通过给你这个，你在做社交网络，你在鸡尾酒会上，现在我们给你一种方式去真正结识新的人或在游戏中改善你的关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, the reason why people, so many, especially middle-aged women love FarmVille and these games was because they had this hobby, but they weren't doing it alone and they were doing it with their good friends, making new friends.
zh: 那么，为什么这么多人，尤其是中年女性喜欢 FarmVille 和这些游戏，是因为她们有这个爱好，但她们不是独自在做，她们是和好朋友一起做，结交新朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in the game, our most successful features were co-op play.
zh: 在游戏中，我们最成功的功能是合作玩法。

speaker: Unknown
en: There were things that let you do gifting and do things of value for your friends.
zh: 有一些功能让你做赠礼并为你的朋友做有价值的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we called the games invest, express and connect.
zh: 所以我们把游戏称为投资、表达和连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could do something that was, you were being seen as creative.
zh: 你可以做一些事情，让你被视为有创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to say people don't want to be creative, they want to feel creative.
zh: 我喜欢说人们不想具有创造力，他们想感觉有创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we were letting you feel creative, look creative to people.
zh: 所以我们让你感觉有创造力，在别人看来有创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's what Midjourney does for me today.
zh: 这就是 Midjourney 今天对我的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we were letting you...
zh: 然后我们让你...

speaker: Unknown
en: So you were expressing yourself and then you were connecting with people around it.
zh: 所以你在表达自己，然后你围绕它与人连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was the first thing.
zh: 所以这是第一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then if you think at a mechanical metric level, our core metric was retention, not virality.
zh: 然后如果你在机械指标层面思考，我们的核心指标是留存，而不是病毒式传播。

speaker: Unknown
en: We grew, we outlasted everybody because we had the best retention.
zh: 我们增长，我们比其他所有人都长久，因为我们有最好的留存。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we were the only consumer company in the world that tracked Day 365 retention.
zh: 我认为我们是世界上唯一一家追踪第 365 天留存的消费类公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think anyone tracks it today.
zh: 我认为今天没有人追踪它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I talk about in the book, I think it's a gift to your founders.
zh: 我在书中谈到了这一点，我认为这是给你们创始人的礼物。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe that the most valuable companies in the world, well, statistically this is true, they have the highest Day 365 retention.
zh: 我相信世界上最有价值的公司，嗯，统计上这是真的，它们拥有最高的第 365 天留存。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can you build against Day 365 retention?
zh: 你能以第 365 天留存为目标来构建吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can't wait till Day 365.
zh: 我们不能等到第 365 天。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can find early indicators of a positive Day 365 and a negative Day 365 retention.
zh: 我们可以找到积极的第 365 天留存和消极的第 365 天留存的早期指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have low D1, low D30, you will probably not have high Day 365.
zh: 如果你的 D1（第 1 天）低，D30（第 30 天）低，你很可能不会有高的第 365 天留存。

speaker: Unknown
en: So those track, those are correlated, but you can have the opposite.
zh: 所以这些追踪，这些是相关的，但你可能有相反的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can have very high Day 30 and a zero Day 365, which is most products.
zh: 你可能有很高的第 30 天留存和零的第 365 天留存，这就是大多数产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you don't think about why someone would use this in a year, if you don't have that mentality, it turns out when someone tries our product, they think that way too.
zh: 如果你不思考为什么有人会在一年后使用它，如果你没有那种心态，结果当有人尝试我们的产品时，他们也会那样想。

speaker: Unknown
en: They kind of think, "Is this worth investing in?"
zh: 他们有点像，"这值得投入吗？"

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether it's a game or a camera, it's, "Am I going to use this in a year?
zh: 无论是游戏还是相机，就是，"我会在一年后用这个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Should I tell my friends about this or should I bother investing?
zh: 我应该告诉我的朋友这个吗，还是我应该费心投入？

speaker: Unknown
en: Should I bother giving it information about me or whatever?"
zh: 我应该费心提供关于我的信息或其他什么吗？"

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think there's a mentality to that.
zh: 我认为这里有一种心态。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like to say viral-based companies, and there's been many, be real.
zh: 我喜欢说基于病毒的公司，有很多，要真实。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've seen these, they're sinking speedboats.
zh: 我们见过这些，它们是正在下沉的快艇。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're trying to drive faster than they're sinking.
zh: 它们试图以比它们下沉更快的速度行驶。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're trying to get more people in.
zh: 他们正试图让更多人进来。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can either go faster, bail water out faster, which is trying to get people back or plug the hole in the boat or build a better boat that doesn't have a hole.
zh: 你要么走得更快，更快地把水舀出去，这是试图让人们回来，要么把船上的洞堵上，要么造一艘没有洞的更好的船。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we were tracking both in our games, in our network, what was the retention?
zh: 但我们在我们的游戏和我们的网络中都在追踪这两者，留存率是多少？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we were really constantly thinking about that.
zh: 我们确实一直在思考这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the two things to take away from Zynga's success in my opinion that I hold is to have a strong innovation vision that translates to what your teams are doing every week.
zh: 我认为从 Zynga 的成功中可以总结出的两点，在我看来我所坚持的是，要有一个强大的创新愿景，这能转化为你的团队每周所做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we even built a metric that's still not used by anyone.
zh: 我们甚至构建了一个至今仍无人使用的指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is an Easter egg if this doesn't get cut and anyone's still listening, we built a metric called ASN and it stood for, we measured what was your active social network.
zh: 这是一个彩蛋，如果这段内容没有被剪辑而且有人还在听的话，我们构建了一个名为 ASN 的指标，它代表的是，我们测量你的活跃社交网络是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we looked at how many round trips you had with a friend or another player, meaning you took a turn and they took a turn back, you gifted them and they gifted you back.
zh: 所以我们查看了你与一个朋友或其他玩家有多少次往返互动，这意味着你走了一步然后他们回了一步，你送了他们礼物然后他们回赠了你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we found that if you went from zero ASN to one, there was an 80% chance we saw you again in the next month.
zh: 我们发现，如果你的 ASN 从零增加到一，我们在下个月再次看到你的可能性是 80%。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if we got you to a four, there was an 80% chance we'd see you 22 out of the next 30 days.
zh: 如果我们让你达到四，我们在接下来的 30 天里有 22 天看到你的可能性是 80%。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we could actually build and innovate against that metric and it made sense because if you saw positive feedback loops, it's like someone likes your photos, comments, that's probably the biggest dopamine hit we get.
zh: 所以我们实际上可以针对该指标进行构建和创新，这是有道理的，因为如果你看到了正向反馈循环，就像有人喜欢你的照片、评论一样，这可能是我们获得的最大多巴胺刺激。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am so excited to tell you about this season's supporting sponsor Vanta.
zh: 我非常激动能告诉你们本季度的支持赞助商 Vanta。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta helps over 15,000 companies like Cursor, Ramp, Duolingo, Snowflake, and Atlassian earn and prove trust with their customers.
zh: Vanta 帮助超过 15,000 家公司，如 Cursor、Ramp、Duolingo、Snowflake 和 Atlassian，赢得并证明其客户的信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams are building and shipping products faster than ever thanks to AI.
zh: 得益于 AI，团队构建和发布产品的速度比以往任何时候都快。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as a result, the amount of risk being introduced into your product and your business is higher than it's ever been.
zh: 但结果就是，引入到你的产品和业务中的风险量比以往任何时候都高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every security leader that I talk to is feeling the increasing weight of protecting their organization, their business, and not to mention their customer data.
zh: 我交谈过的每一位安全领导者都感受到了保护其组织、其业务，更不用说其客户数据的日益沉重的压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because things are moving so fast, they are constantly reacting, having to guess at priorities, and having to make do with outdated solutions.
zh: 因为事物发展太快，他们不断做出反应，不得不猜测优先级，不得不凑合使用过时的解决方案。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta automates compliance and risk management with over 35 security and privacy frameworks, including SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA.
zh: Vanta 通过超过 35 个安全和隐私框架自动化合规和风险管理，包括 SOC 2、ISO 27001 和 HIPAA。

speaker: Unknown
en: This helps companies get compliant fast and stay compliant.
zh: 这有助于公司快速合规并保持合规。

speaker: Unknown
en: More than ever before, trust has the power to make or break your business.
zh: 与以往相比，信任更有力量决定你的业务的成败。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn more at vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 了解更多信息请访问 vanta.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a listener of this podcast, you get $1,000 off Vanta.
zh: 作为本播客的听众，你可以获得 Vanta 1000 美元的优惠。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 vanta.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about building a consumer social app.
zh: 让我们谈谈构建一个消费者社交应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very hard.
zh: 非常难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very few people have successfully done it and built something durable.
zh: 很少有人成功做到并构建出了持久的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talk about this on the podcast a bunch.
zh: 我们在播客中经常讨论这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like nothing works in consumer.
zh: 这就像没有什么东西在消费者领域是有效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It works for a little bit and then dies.
zh: 它起作用了一小会儿然后就死掉了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you maybe built more social consumer products than anyone.
zh: 而且你可能比其他人都构建了更多的社交消费者产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's like a dozen that have worked.
zh: 大概只有十几个是成功的。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI obviously changes the game in theory.
zh: 显而易见，AI 在理论上改变了游戏规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you think needs to be true for something to work again in today's world?
zh: 你认为要想在当今世界让某种东西再次奏效，必须具备什么条件？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably equally surprising to you how little we're seeing in social and consumer in general.
zh: 我们在社交和消费者领域总体上看到的东西如此之少，这可能同样让你感到惊讶。

speaker: Unknown
en: It seems like people have given up on the idea that a social app could take off or be viral and I see them every so often and they've given up for good reason because nothing's proven and nothing's working.
zh: 人们似乎已经放弃了社交应用能够腾飞或具有病毒性的想法，我偶尔会看到它们，而且他们放弃是有充分理由的，因为没有任何东西被证明有效，没有任何东西在起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I like to think first, is there latent demand?
zh: 所以我首先喜欢思考，是否存在潜在需求？

speaker: Unknown
en: So if just because a category doesn't exist today, it doesn't mean that we don't want it as consumers.
zh: 所以仅仅因为一个类别今天不存在，并不意味着我们作为消费者不想要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, the best opportunities are usually the opposite.
zh: 事实上，最好的机会通常是相反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in 2007 when I started Zynga, video gaming was a $23 billion business, but I had stopped playing games.
zh: 2007 年当我创办 Zynga 时，电子游戏是一个价值 230 亿美元的生意，但我已经停止玩游戏了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't know anyone who played games.
zh: 我不认识任何玩游戏的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like not even a top 10 activity on the web and I...
zh: 这甚至不是网上的前十项活动，我……

speaker: Unknown
en: ... A top 10 activity on the web.
zh: ……网上的前十项活动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I thought there's a latent demand.
zh: 我认为存在潜在需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love games.
zh: 我喜欢游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love playing with my family, but I can't get people to play games with me and it's too much investment.
zh: 我喜欢和家人一起玩，但我没法让人们陪我玩游戏，而且投入太大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's too hard.
zh: 太难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was crawling across broken glass to get someone to play a game with you.
zh: 想要让人陪玩游戏就像在碎玻璃上爬行一样艰难。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got my nephews who are intense gamers to play Rise of Nations with me and we set up two machines for them and two for me and we had FaceTime going on a machine and then the game and we were on a game server and we did that one time.
zh: 我让我那些狂热的玩家侄子们陪我玩《国家的崛起》，我们为他们设置了两台机器，也为我设置了两台，我们在一台机器上开着 FaceTime，然后还有游戏，我们在游戏服务器上，我们只做了一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "That is a lot of effort."
zh: 我说，“那太费劲了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was fun, but we did it once.
zh: 那很有趣，但我们只做了一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: My premise was, "Okay, I believe this could be and should be a mass market activity.
zh: 我的前提是，“好吧，我相信这可能并且应该是一种大众市场活动。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe adults want to give themselves permission to play, but I got to make it so accessible, so cheap in terms of what it asks for them."
zh: 我相信成年人想要给自己许可去玩耍，但我必须让它非常易于上手，并且就它对他们提出的要求而言非常便宜。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Free, not just free event, which it wasn't.
zh: 免费，不仅仅是免费活动，虽然它并不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was $60 to go buy a game.
zh: 要花 60 美元去买个游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I made it free and I said, "I'm going to make it a game you already know.
zh: 所以我把它做成了免费的，我说，“我要让它成为一个你已经知道的游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: Three clicks and you're in and I'm going to ask five to 15 minutes of you to do this.
zh: 点三下你就进去了，我只会占用你 5 到 15 分钟来做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're going to trip over it.
zh: 你会偶然撞上它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a breadcrumb somewhere.
zh: 它是某处的一个面包屑。

speaker: Unknown
en: Say we don't go to the comic book store, but if the comics were in the newspaper, we'd read them."
zh: 假设我们不去漫画书店，但如果漫画在报纸里，我们就会阅读。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I believed there was a latent demand.
zh: 所以我相信存在一种潜在需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, today gaming is a $280 billion industry.
zh: 事实上，如今游戏是一个价值 2800 亿美元的产业。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I actually think there's latent demand.
zh: 我确实认为存在潜在需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, I don't play games.
zh: 再说一次，我不玩游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one I know plays games other than my nieces and nephews.
zh: 除了我的侄子和侄女，我认识的人里没有人玩游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet it's this big a business.
zh: 然而它却是这么大的生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I actually think there's another massive growth waiting in games because they're this big and it's this boring and adults aren't doing it or they must be some.
zh: 所以我确实认为游戏界还有另一个巨大的增长在等待，因为它已经这么大，而且这么无聊，成年人也不在做，或者他们一定有一些人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very big, but none that I know.
zh: 它非常大，但我认识的人里没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so social and consumer.
zh: 好的，那么社交和消费者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe that we have beyond a latent demand for social, we are being social online.
zh: 我相信我们对社交不仅有潜在需求，我们正在网上进行社交。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are on Snapchat and Instagram and TikTok, but I believe it's lost the adrenaline.
zh: 我们在 Snapchat、Instagram 和 TikTok 上，但我相信它已经失去了肾上腺素。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot about adrenaline.
zh: 我经常思考肾上腺素。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a heat in it?
zh: 里面有热度吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you excited to go get on Instagram or do you feel a little bit like you're eating potato chips?
zh: 你很兴奋去上 Instagram 还是感觉有点像你在吃薯片？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are these positive calories or negative calories or empty calories in the middle?
zh: 这些是积极的热量还是消极的热量，或者是中间的空热量？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think one interesting thing, we looked at NPS, Net Promoter Scores and we saw that when people quit Facebook and quit Instagram, they went from a plus 35 to a negative 35.
zh: 我觉得有一件有趣的事，我们查看了 NPS，净推荐值，我们看到当人们离开 Facebook 和离开 Instagram 时，他们的得分从正 35 变成了负 35。

speaker: Unknown
en: People have a feeling like they just quit smoking.
zh: 人们有一种感觉就像他们刚戒烟一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are proud to tell you they're not on Instagram.
zh: 人们很自豪地告诉你他们不在 Instagram 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not missing the party.
zh: 他们并没有错过派对。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Whoa, I got off it."
zh: 他们会说，“哇，我把它戒掉了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think all that's to say, I think it's the biggest unexplored opportunity there is on the internet.
zh: 所以我想所有这一切都是为了说明，我认为这是互联网上最大的未开发机遇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel very confident that someone is going to reinvent this social experience for the agentic AI age, but how are they going to do it?
zh: 我非常有信心，有人会为 agentic AI 时代重新发明这种社交体验，但他们要怎么做呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: I would guess that they're going to give us productivity again, that they're going ... Part of what we got, people forget Facebook gave us massive social productivity.
zh: 我猜他们会再次给我们带来生产力，他们会…… 我们得到的一部分东西，人们忘了 Facebook 给了我们巨大的社交生产力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were able to stay in the loop with 300 or 1,000 friends.
zh: 我们能够与 300 或 1000 个朋友保持联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: LinkedIn gave us massive productivity and they still do, but they started to move away from that productivity into a realm of wasting time because they wanted to engage more and have more ads and less LinkedIn, but they might have ads now, but definitely Instagram had TikTok envy so they started doing that.
zh: LinkedIn 给了我们巨大的生产力，他们依然如此，但他们开始从那种生产力转移到浪费时间的领域，因为他们想要更多的参与度和更多的广告以及更少的 LinkedIn，但他们现在可能有广告了，但绝对是 Instagram 嫉妒 TikTok 所以他们开始那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there is a new step function of productivity that we could be getting in that experience that our agents are letting us stay even more in the loop with the people we care about, but not wasting our time as much.
zh: 所以我认为我们在那种体验中可以获得一个新的生产力阶跃函数，我们的智能体正让我们与我们关心的人保持更紧密的联系，但不会那么浪费我们的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll also say this, there's a side of this, I call this social thing the cocktail party.
zh: 我还要说一点，这方面有一个侧面，我把这个社交事物称为鸡尾酒会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the big instinct vein for me.
zh: 这对我来说是主要的直觉脉络。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think about that cocktail party and I think where is that happening or where does it want to happen and how do we add things to the cocktail?
zh: 我思考那个鸡尾酒会，我想它正在哪里发生，或者它想要在哪里发生，以及我们要如何向鸡尾酒中添加东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'd love to host the cocktail party and if we're not, we'd love to be at it.
zh: 我们很乐意主办这个鸡尾酒会，如果我们不是主办，我们很乐意出席。

speaker: Unknown
en: We know it when we see a great cocktail party, you know it.
zh: 当我们看到一个很棒的鸡尾酒会时我们就知道了，你知道的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You feel it, you're like, "Oh, I'm so glad I'm here."
zh: 你能感觉到它，你会想，“噢，我很高兴我在这里。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it moves from obligatory, I'm at this party, it's the birthday party to almost like greed like, "I love this party.
zh: 它从强制性，我在这个派对，这是一个生日派对，变成了几乎像贪婪一样，“我喜欢这个派对。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the people I'm meeting."
zh: 我喜欢我遇见的人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And what do you get a great cocktail party?
zh: 你能从一个很棒的鸡尾酒会得到什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You often get great leads.
zh: 你通常会得到很好的线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that cocktail party that for me, it started with Napster when all of a sudden all of us were connected to each other and the great lead was a music file, but then Friendster, Facebook, LinkedIn, what we were getting was better lead generation.
zh: 所以那个鸡尾酒会对我来说，它始于 Napster，那时突然之间我们所有人都连接在了一起，而那个很棒的线索就是一个音乐文件，但是 Friendster、Facebook、LinkedIn，我们得到的是更好的线索生成。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was better productivity for our time.
zh: 那对我们的时间来说是更好的生产力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before that, we would go to Google for a lead or Craigslist and it was a lot of noise to signal and now we're like, "Oh my God, I could get on Friendster and find a date and it's actually a good lead.
zh: 在那之前，我们会去 Google 寻找线索或者 Craigslist，那里噪音很多信号很少，现在我们会想，“天哪，我可以在 Friendster 上找个约会，这实际上是个好线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had a good date on Friendster."
zh: 我在 Friendster 上有过一次不错的约会。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I would say to people is like, look, if you want to reinvent social, look for where the cocktail is or you could host and then think about how is lead generation happening.
zh: 所以我想对人们说，看，如果你想重塑社交，去寻找鸡尾酒会在哪，或者你可以主持，然后思考线索生成是如何发生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it might sound weird when I say lead gen, but what is LinkedIn?
zh: 当我说线索生成时听起来可能很怪，但 LinkedIn 是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: LinkedIn start with lead gen. All the productivity, the value started in a utility and being in this cocktail party.
zh: LinkedIn 以线索生成开始。所有的生产力，价值始于一个实用性，并处在这个鸡尾酒会上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today and by the way, with Zynga, I said, "I'm going to go to the cocktail party.
zh: 今天顺便说一句，在 Zynga 时，我说，我要去那个鸡尾酒会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's hanging out on social networks.
zh: 每个人都挂在社交网络上。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to drop games in the middle and give them a new dimension to network in the games because they want to be on Facebook.
zh: 我要把游戏丢到中间，给他们一个新的维度在网络中在游戏里，因为他们想待在 Facebook 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to give them more to do."
zh: 我想给他们更多事情做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today we're all hanging out on our Claude on our GPT, but there's no cocktail party.
zh: 今天我们都挂在我们的 Claude 我们的 GPT 上，但没有鸡尾酒会。

speaker: Unknown
en: So my Easter egg people is, it's a quiet, lonely cocktail party like the web was before social networking.
zh: 所以我的彩蛋是，它是一个安静、孤独的鸡尾酒会，就像社交网络之前的网络那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my challenge to your listeners is figure out how to make it rowdy, figure out how to make that cocktail party social and socially productive and you will probably find gold there.
zh: 我对听众的挑战是弄清楚如何让它变得喧闹，弄清楚如何让那个鸡尾酒会变得社交化并具有社交生产力，你可能会在那里发现金子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to come back to your thread on your question was how do you know when an idea is a B plus?
zh: 我想回到你的线索关于你的问题是你如何知道一个想法是 B+？

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the question on, well, how do you know if it's a B plus?
zh: 我喜欢这个问题，好吧，你怎么知道它是 B+？

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I would say to you is, how do you know if you're with the right person?
zh: 我想对你说的是，你怎么知道你和正确的人在一起？

speaker: Unknown
en: What was your experience dating?
zh: 你约会时的体验是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And my experience has been that when I'm with the right person, I know it.
zh: 我的体验是，当我和正确的人在一起，我知道它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not asking, "Is this the right person?"
zh: 我不是在问，这是正确的人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Fuck yeah, I love this person.
zh: 我就像，去他的，是的，我爱这个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is my person and that's the best feeling ever.
zh: 这是我的那个人，那是最好的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I hope everyone can experience that."
zh: 我希望每个人都能体验那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you're with the A minus person, you're with the B+, you're asking, "Could this be the one?"
zh: 当你和 A- 的人在一起，你和 B+ 在一起，你在问，这可能是那个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And Osho said, "If you're torn between two paths, they're both wrong.
zh: 奥修说，如果你在两条路之间纠结，它们都错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have feet in two canoes.
zh: 你的脚在两条独木舟里。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're asking whether or not your product is an A, it's not an A and you're full of hope.
zh: 如果你在问你的产品是不是 A，它不是 A 并且你充满了希望。

speaker: Unknown
en: You hope it's an A.
zh: 你希望它是 A。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't mean you might not have the chance to turn that into an A, but the first point of intellectual honesty is to say, okay, it's not."
zh: 这并不意味着你可能没有机会把它变成 A，但是智力诚实的第一点是说，好吧，它不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, well, how do you know how are you getting validation that it's not?
zh: 然后，好吧，你怎么知道你如何获得验证它不是？

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have the validation that it is.
zh: 你没有验证它是。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you have lightning in a bottle, when you have true signal, everything works.
zh: 当你拥有瓶中闪电，当你拥有真正的信号，一切都有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's anecdotally, you love your product, you're addicted to it.
zh: 这是轶闻，你爱你的产品，你对它上瘾。

speaker: Unknown
en: You show it to friends and they love it.
zh: 你把它展示给朋友，他们爱它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your metrics show that it works.
zh: 你的指标显示它有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did we have to ask if GPT was it?
zh: 我们不得不问 GPT 是否是它吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Did we say, "Oh, is GPT it?
zh: 我们说，噢，GPT 是它吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not sure."
zh: 我不确定。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're like, "No, I'm fucking living on that and it's making me dream of new things to do."
zh: 我们就像，不，我就靠那活着并且它让我梦想着做新的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'd say that the hard part about this is what do you do with a B plus?
zh: 所以我会说这困难的部分是你对 B+ 做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: First, can we be intellectually honest that it is a B plus?
zh: 第一，我们能否在智力上诚实它是 B+？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what do we do with a B+?
zh: 然后我们对 B+ 做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we just kill it and start all over again?
zh: 我们就杀掉它并重新开始吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we use it to learn?
zh: 我们用它来学习吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: The power is knowing it's a B plus.
zh: 力量在于知道它是 B+。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the first point is, " Okay, this is not it."
zh: 第一点是，好吧，这不是它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now what do I do with that?
zh: 现在我用它做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it a way to learn?
zh: 它是一种学习的方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: D I see examples that are near this, that are it?
zh: 我看到接近这个的例子，它是它吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I find anything proven or near proven?
zh: 我能找到任何被证明或接近被证明的东西吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: When we realize it's not it, we look for more things to do and it's such a hard lesson.
zh: 当我们意识到它不是它，我们寻找更多的事情做，这是如此艰难的一课。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two weeks ago, I pulled the plug on my do.Earth project for the fourth time.
zh: 两周前，我第四次终止了我的 do.Earth 项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been building my version of the metaverse for 20 years.
zh: 我构建我的元宇宙版本已经 20 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's what led me to Zynga.
zh: 这就是让我去 Zynga 的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I call the metaverse that I want life at the speed of play.
zh: 我把我想要的元宇宙称为以游戏速度生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're getting closer and closer to that anyway with AI.
zh: 无论如何，我们正随着 AI 越来越接近这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, I define the metaverse and I give Reid Hoffman credit for this definition as blurring the lines between the virtual and the reel.
zh: 顺便说一句，我定义元宇宙——我把这个定义归功于 Reid Hoffman——就是模糊虚拟与现实之间的界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is where our lives are going anyway.
zh: 这也是我们要去的生活方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're getting more and more metaverse in.
zh: 所以我们正在加入越来越多的元宇宙。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not being in this other escape to immersive world.
zh: 它不是要进入这个逃避现实的沉浸式世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that the virtual and the reel completely blur together, but I kept coming at it from the game side and I went native, not in the good way.
zh: 而是虚拟与现实完全模糊在一起，但我一直是从游戏这边切入，然后我陷进去了，不是那种好的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got so into wanting to prove I could build this experience that I was building this game engine, web browser-based game engine and I was not getting a product market fit.
zh: 我太想证明我能构建这种体验，以至于我构建了游戏引擎、基于浏览器的游戏引擎，但我没有获得产品市场契合。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was being too ambitious.
zh: 我太雄心勃勃了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was not going for a small, small idea.
zh: 我没有去追求一个很小的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was going for a really big idea and it was really hard and I pulled the plug on it after four years and $25 million in just this one version of it.
zh: 我追求的是一个真正宏大的想法，那真的很难，在四年和仅仅这一个版本就投入 2500 万美元之后，我终止了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in the last two weeks since then, I've been more inspired around ideas than I have been in four years.
zh: 而在自那之后的两周里，我对想法的灵感比过去四年里都要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there is power in pulling the plug on a B+ and even more on a D.
zh: 所以终止一个 B+ 级的项目是有力量的，终止一个 D 级项目更是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a whole discussion I want to have about the abyss, which you talk a lot about in the book, about how that's such a source of good ideas.
