zh: 我想有一场关于「深渊」的讨论，你在书里谈了很多关于「深渊」的内容，关于它是如何成为好想法的源泉。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I want to touch on distribution just as we're on this thread.
zh: 但既然我们在聊这个话题，我想谈谈分发。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something that's come up a lot on this podcast is just how with AI making it so easy to build stuff.
zh: 这个播客中经常出现的一个话题就是，随着 AI 让构建东西变得如此容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the biggest challenges becomes distribution.
zh: 最大的挑战之一变成了分发。

speaker: Unknown
en: Always been a challenge, always been hard, harder than ever, because there's so much happening in the market constantly, so much trying to get your attention.
zh: 它一直是个挑战，一直很难，比以往任何时候都难，因为市场一直在发生这么多事，有这么多东西在争夺你的注意力。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the channels are full paid and SEO.
zh: 所有的渠道都是付费和 SEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your just advice to founders?
zh: 你对创始人的建议是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you think needs to be true for a startup to break out?
zh: 你认为一个初创公司要突围需要具备什么条件？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because it's so hard now.
zh: 因为现在太难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's start with talking about consumer distribution, which I understand better.
zh: 我们先从消费者分发开始谈起，这个我更了解。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's one question we got to back up and really explore, which is AI a new platform?
zh: 我们必须退后一步真正探讨一个问题，那就是 AI 是一个新的平台吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I would argue that it is not yet a new platform.
zh: 我认为它还不是一个新平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is an important technology.
zh: 它是一项重要的技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have a new kind of portal to that in GPT or whatever chat we use, AI chat, but it's not in my mind a platform.
zh: 我们有一个通往它的新入口，在 GPT 或者我们使用的任何聊天、AI 聊天中，但在我心目中它不是一个平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: A platform in the traditional sense of it was a hardware platform.
zh: 传统意义上的平台是一个硬件平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, it's definitely not a hardware platform at the consumer side yet.
zh: 嗯，它在消费端肯定还不是一个硬件平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are trying to explore that, but it is not yet.
zh: 人们正在尝试探索这一点，但它还不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it became at least an interface platform, whether it was a Windows interface or a browser interface or a social network that opened up.
zh: 而后它至少变成了一个接口平台，无论是 Windows 接口还是浏览器接口，还是开放出来的社交网络。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in the case of mobile, it was both hardware and an interface.
zh: 在移动端的情况下，它既是硬件也是接口。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not at that point yet.
zh: 我们还没到那一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe we will get there.
zh: 我相信我们会到那一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know how I have ideas, but we're not there yet.
zh: 我不知道我有些什么想法，但我们还没到。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's important to acknowledge that and say we're still in the mobile and web era.
zh: 承认这一点并说我们仍处于移动和 web 时代是很重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We still use a browser.
zh: 我们仍然使用浏览器。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even though we're in this chat app, it is not a platform for other apps and experiences and developers.
zh: 即使我们在使用这个聊天应用，它也不是其他应用、体验和开发者的平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be.
zh: 它可能是。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hope it becomes one, but it's not yet.
zh: 我希望它变成一个，但它还不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're kind of like halfway to a platform.
zh: 我们就像是处于平台的半途。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are in a point of discovery.
zh: 我们正处于一个发现的阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: New technologies that break through at the consumer level unlock discovery.
zh: 在消费者层面突破的新技术会解锁发现。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's really important.
zh: 这真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Discovery means when we first got our phones, we were installing new apps all the time.
zh: 发现意味着当我们第一次拿到手机时，我们一直在安装新应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now we don't ever install new apps.
zh: 现在我们不再安装新应用了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The average app installs per user per month is zero.
zh: 每位用户每月的平均应用安装量是零。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's really important to think about that, that not only are we not in discovery, but even when we are, it doesn't stick.
zh: 所以考虑到这一点非常重要，即我们不仅不在发现阶段，即使我们在，也无法持续。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was something like 40,000 new games launched last year in the app store and zero became top 10 hits and zero even sustained top 25 or 50.
zh: 去年应用商店大约推出了 40,000 款新游戏，没有一款进入前十名，甚至没有一款保持在前 25 或 50 名。

speaker: Unknown
en: So your odds are bad.
zh: 所以你的胜算很渺茫。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's not an ideal moment to be ... You can't launch consumer right now with high confidence.
zh: 所以现在不是一个理想的时刻……你不能现在高置信度地推出消费者产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's just something that we have to acknowledge and it makes consumer and consumer categories like social and games almost not investible.
zh: 这是我们必须承认的事情，这使得消费者和消费者类别（如社交和游戏）几乎不可投资。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're still getting investment, but it's very hard.
zh: 它们仍在获得投资，但这非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's much easier to be prosumer or enterprise now, but for good reason because we're seeing those companies scale revenue ARRs very quickly.
zh: 现在做专业消费者或企业业务要容易得多，但这是有充分理由的，因为我们看到这些公司的收入和年度经常性收入（ARR）增长非常快。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I do think it's a great time to be working on consumer ideas.
zh: 所以我确实认为现在是从事消费者创意的好时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you asked about distribution.
zh: 你刚才问到了分发。

speaker: Unknown
en: Distribution is just so crucial and core to any consumer, any business idea, but any consumer idea, and it can't be an afterthought.
zh: 分发对于任何消费者、任何商业理念，但特别是任何消费者理念都至关重要且处于核心地位，它不能是事后诸葛亮。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not, I'm going to build the best mousetrap and they're going to come.
zh: 不是说，“我要造出最好的捕鼠器，然后他们就会来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Distribution has to be part of your product and part of baked into the strategy deeply and proven from the beginning.
zh: 分发必须是你产品的一部分，必须深深地融入战略之中，并从一开始就得到证明。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're just building this product and hoping they will come, hoping it'll spread virally or word of mouth, that's hope strategy, not a belief strategy.
zh: 如果你只是构建这个产品并希望他们来，希望它能像病毒一样传播或通过口碑传播，那是一种希望策略，而不是信念策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it doesn't mean you shouldn't keep experimenting and now is a great time to experiment.
zh: 这并不意味着你不应该继续尝试，现在是一个尝试的好时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, it'll be a hundred times more expensive to try to get the same things in the market in a year because it'll be a hundred times more noise.
zh: 事实上，一年后在市场上尝试获得同样的东西，成本会高出 100 倍，因为噪音会多 100 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And once there is distribution, there'll be a flood of competition.
zh: 一旦有了分发渠道，就会有大量竞争涌入。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when distribution is this broken, it is even more core to your product and your strategy.
zh: 但是当分发如此糟糕时，它对你的产品和战略来说更加核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we're seeing many more successful companies that find a prosumer approach, they go after their power users, their whales, the people who care enough to find it, care enough to spend money on it upfront to sustain a business long before we can get out to consumer.
zh: 我认为我们看到更多成功的公司找到了一种专业消费者的方法，他们追求他们的核心用户、他们的鲸鱼用户，那些足够关心去找到它、足够关心去预先花钱支持它的人，以便在我们能够接触到消费者之前长期维持业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is a great idea that Garry Tan had that I'll repeat here because I loved it so much this week, that if we get in the mindset of basically intelligence on tap, free tokens and if we think that the amount of tokens that we're buying today, that will basically be free in a year or two, that the token supply is going to go up so much.
zh: Garry Tan 有一个很棒的想法，我要在这里重复一下，因为这周我太喜欢它了，那就是如果我们进入基本上是“即插即用的智能”、免费代币的心态，如果我们认为我们今天购买的代币数量，在一两年后基本上是免费的，代币供应量将会大幅增加。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our demand will too, but if today we start building, re-imagining consumer services based on free tokens, I think that's such an interesting innovation zone and it could sound like a dot-com business plan when I remember ordering pig ears from my dog, Zynga, and wanting to send thank you notes to the VCs because they were cheaper than at Petco.
zh: 我们的需求也会增加，但如果今天我们开始基于免费代币构建、重新构想消费者服务，我认为这是一个非常有趣的创新领域，这听起来可能像是一个互联网泡沫时代的商业计划，我记得那时给我的狗 Zynga 订猪耳朵，并想给风险投资人们写感谢信，因为它们比 Petco 的便宜。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not a good business plan.
zh: 那不是一个好的商业计划。

speaker: Unknown
en: So giving away tokens at a loss isn't a good business plan, but it might be in a phenomenal business because what the dotcom thing had wrong was the price of pig ears wasn't going to come down or delivery wasn't going to come down, but the price of tokens will.
zh: 所以亏本送代币不是一个好的商业计划，但它可能是一门非凡的生意，因为互联网泡沫那件事做错的地方在于猪耳朵的价格不会下降，或者配送成本不会下降，但代币的价格会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that's another kind of Easter egg of an insight that I'm intrigued by and I think founders are going to do something really interesting with it because games don't work, freemium games and freemium apps that have to go and hit the AI that even if you wanted to spend on it, you're hitting token limits.
zh: 所以我认为这是另一个让我感兴趣的见解彩蛋，我认为创始人们会利用它做一些真正有趣的事情，因为游戏行不通，必须接触 AI 的免费增值游戏和免费增值应用，即使你想在上面花钱，你也会遇到代币限制。

speaker: Unknown
en: So getting around that today might unlock really interesting innovation.
zh: 所以今天绕过这一点可能会解锁真正有趣的创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also just another Easter egg thought on the social cocktail party.
zh: 此外，关于社交鸡尾酒会，还有一个彩蛋式的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the idea of the agent being in the middle on brokering our social relationships and getting back to that lead gen, dating, jobs, general listings.
zh: 我喜欢智能体作为中间人介入来撮合我们的社交关系，并回到线索生成、约会、工作、一般分类信息上的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I love the idea that the agent has our context and knows my context and your context and we have this membrane, I call it the social membrane, this intelligent membrane of trust and it gets smarter and smarter at knowing when to change dynamically change that trust or change that attention level that it's going to take from us like a great chief of staff and it could share less information with both of us than it knows.
zh: 但我更喜欢这样的想法：智能体拥有我们的背景，知道我的背景和你的背景，我们之间有一层膜，我称之为社交膜，这层信任的智能膜变得越来越智能，知道何时动态地改变那种信任，或者改变它要从我们这里获取的关注程度，就像一位伟大的幕僚长，它分享给我们的信息可能比它知道的要少。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be you're in Marin and I'm like, "Lenny, let's hang out."
zh: 可能是你在 Marin，我就像说，“Lenny，咱们聚聚吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, "Mark, I'd love that."
zh: 你就像说，“Mark，我很乐意。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So okay, let's have our social secretaries work on that.
zh: 所以好吧，让我们让社交秘书来处理这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's going to be an asymmetry in your interest and my interest in any given weekend and hanging out and how do we broker that without hurting one of our feelings or going into ...
zh: 在任何特定的周末，对聚会的兴趣上，你的兴趣和我的兴趣之间会存在不对称，我们如何在不伤害彼此感情或不陷入……的情况下撮合这一点？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's some really interesting spaces that the AI and the agent can broker between us in ways that start to give us both productivity.
zh: 有一些非常有趣的空间，AI 和智能体可以在我们之间进行撮合，这种方式开始让我们双方都提高效率。

speaker: Unknown
en: They find ways for us to get together without getting in a bad place of you see me on your calendar for Saturday and you want to be with your kids.
zh: 它们找到让我们在一起的方法，而不会陷入糟糕的境地，比如你在日历上看到周六有我，但你却想陪孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love all these ideas you're sharing.
zh: 我喜欢你分享的这些所有想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'm hearing there, there's this always this idea of why can't we just, here's my calendar, here's your calendar, let's just find time.
zh: 我在那听到的是，总是有这样的想法，为什么我们不能，这是我的日历，那是你的日历，我们就找个时间吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the issue there is you don't want to ever feel like you don't want to look like you've got nothing going on and you're like the most boring person.
zh: 那里的问题是你不想感觉像是你不想看起来你无所事事，而你就像最无聊的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't want the other person to know.
zh: 你不想让对方知道。

speaker: Mark Pincus
en: Yeah, it's totally open, available to you.
zh: 是的，完全开放，随时有空。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，确切地说。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even worse, you don't want it to schedule you for a meeting that was not your party.
zh: 更糟糕的是，你不想它为你安排一个不是你方的会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's right.
zh: 是的，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just to close the loop on the distribution part, what I'm hearing is we might be on the verge of a new distribution platform, a way to get out and for people to discover you.
zh: 为了结束分发这个话题，我听到的是我们可能正处于一个新的分发平台、一种走出去让人们发现你的方式的边缘。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not there yet.
zh: 它还不存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm somewhere between belief and hope in this because I don't have enough evidence to believe it and it's a little more likely to me than just hope.
zh: 在这个问题上，我介于相信和希望之间，因为我没有足够的证据去相信它，而且对我来说它比单纯的希望更有可能一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think right now there's a obvious race and competition around coding between the major LLMs.
zh: 我认为现在在主要的大型语言模型之间，围绕编码存在着明显的竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think they're going to get back to consumer and prosumer and developers' hearts and minds.
zh: 我认为他们会回到消费者、专业消费者以及开发者的心智。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think they are interested in developers' hopes and minds, but first they're competing on this coding front.
zh: 我认为他们对开发者的希望和想法感兴趣，但他们首先在这个编码战线上竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think where we will get to one way or another is that there will be some kind of consumer facing platform for AI apps and agents.
zh: 但我认为我们迟早会达到某种程度，即会有某种面向消费者的 AI 应用和智能体平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can see so many consumer facing agentic services that make so much sense to me that I think consumers will want, but they're not going to get it because if it's just buried in ...
zh: 我能看到如此多面向消费者的智能体服务对我来说非常合理，我认为消费者会想要，但他们不会得到它，因为如果它只是被埋没在……

speaker: Unknown
en: I doubt they're going to get it buried in the mobile app store.
zh: 我怀疑他们不会在移动应用商店的深处得到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's going to be in OpenAI's interest, Claude, Grok to see these apps, consumer facing agentic service apps happen and see them be successful with their LLM baked in.
zh: 我认为这符合 OpenAI、Claude、Grok 的利益，看到这些应用，面向消费者的智能体服务应用发生并看到它们集成了他们的 LLM 而取得成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the more it's uniquely instantiated to OpenAI, the more it drives their consumer value proposition.
zh: 越是针对 OpenAI 独特实例化，就越能推动他们的消费者价值主张。

speaker: Unknown
en: One example that is my number one is my Easter egg on this is I think there should be an agentic travel agent.
zh: 我的第一名例子是我在这里的彩蛋，我认为应该有一个智能体旅行代理人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So a perfect example to me of a place where I think there is latent demand and the only reason we don't have it is because it doesn't make economic sense to offer it to us because we won't pay enough for it is a travel agent.
zh: 所以对我来说一个完美的例子，我认为存在潜在需求，而我们没有它的唯一原因是，因为提供它在经济上不合理，因为我们不会为此支付足够的钱，那就是旅行代理人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe if I offered you a free 24/7 travel agent that was always there for you, knows your travel context, knows you, and will actually not just book travel, but I think the most valuable part is when you're in the middle of a travel, a trip, be ready to rebook your flight and be on top of your travel logistics as they're happening and often failing.
zh: 我相信如果我为你提供一个免费的 24/7 旅行代理人，它永远为你服务，知道你的旅行背景，了解你，并且实际上不仅会预订旅行，而且我认为最有价值的部分是当你旅行中途，旅行途中，准备好为你重新预订航班并掌握你的旅行物流，因为它们正在发生并且经常失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's valuable to all of us but not available to all of us because travel agents couldn't make a living.
zh: 我认为这对我们所有人都有价值，但并非对我们所有人可用，因为旅行代理人无法谋生。

speaker: Unknown
en: They weren't getting enough commissions and we weren't ready to pay enough.
zh: 他们没有得到足够的佣金，而我们也没有准备好支付足够的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the internet has grown through us getting a better deal, but I think that the next area of growth probably will include deals, but I think it's going to be about getting amazing services and that's my prime example.
zh: 所以互联网通过我们要到更优惠的交易而增长，但我认为下一个增长领域可能包括交易，但我认为这将是获得惊人的服务，这是我的主要例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if someone built the best travel agent today, maybe we'd find out about it, but it's a tough road.
zh: 但如果有人今天建立了最好的旅行代理人，也许我们会发现它，但这是一条艰难的道路。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if it was specifically baked into OpenAI or Claude and gave me a real clear reason that I'm getting value from my GPT beyond ...
zh: 但如果它是专门集成到 OpenAI 或 Claude 中，并给我一个真正清晰的理由，让我从我的 GPT 中获得价值，而不是……

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of generic value I get through AI chat today that I'm having more and more trouble differentiating and it's just I don't even know anymore if I'm asking a question to Claude or to GPT.
zh: 我今天通过 AI 聊天获得了很多通用价值，我越来越难以区分它们，我只是不知道了，我是向 Claude 还是向 GPT 提问。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so they at some point, and that's why they're differentiating themselves right now on coding and there's real value and that's an amazing business, but they're not differentiating themselves very much at the consumer level.
zh: 所以他们在某个时候，这就是为什么他们现在在编码方面将自己差异化，并且有真正的价值，这是一项惊人的业务，但他们在消费者层面上没有将自己很好地差异化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: To me, the question is, are they just going to do it or why do they need anyone to do it if they are building this AGI they could just do all the things.
zh: 对我来说，问题是，他们只是要去做吗，或者如果他们正在构建这个 AGI，他们可以做所有事情，为什么需要任何人来做呢。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's going to be interesting to see if they just want to eat the entire market of everything where they-
zh: 所以我认为看看他们是否想要吃掉整个万物市场将会很有趣，他们……

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's going to be interesting to see if they just want to eat the entire market of everything where they play nice with other startups.
zh: 所以我认为看看他们是否想要吃掉整个万物市场并与其它初创公司友好相处将会很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that is going to be interesting.
zh: 是的，那将会很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And look, we can see past examples with Microsoft.
zh: 而且，我们可以看到过去的微软例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Microsoft was a brutal monopoly.
zh: 微软是一个残酷的垄断者。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were a brutal platform.
zh: 他们是一个残酷的平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: They ate everything around them.
zh: 他们吃掉了周围的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then with us, we saw it on Facebook.
zh: 然后对于我们，我们在 Facebook 上看到了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: The two areas that they just didn't care about going into was music, because it was really painful with IP rights, and games, because they didn't want to hire artists.
zh: 他们根本不关心的两个领域是音乐，因为知识产权版权真的很痛苦，以及游戏，因为他们不想雇佣艺术家。

speaker: Unknown
en: But everything else they did absorb.
zh: 但他们吸收了其他所有东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to go in a different direction and touch on... You have all these really amazing nuggets of advice for scaling company, managing people, hiring people, working with people.
zh: 我想换个方向并谈谈……你有所有这些非常棒的建议，关于扩展公司、管理人员、雇佣人员、与人合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to just touch on a bunch of these.
zh: 我只是想涉及其中的一些。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is this idea of making everybody a CEO at your company.
zh: 一个是在你的公司让每个人都成为 CEO 的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that lesson.
zh: 谈谈那个教训。

speaker: Unknown
en: All my management principles I got to through desperation.
zh: 我所有的管理原则都是通过绝望得来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't like managing people.
zh: 我不喜欢管理人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: I say, "Every day you're managing people is a day of work."
zh: 我说，“你管理人员的每一天就是工作的一天。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's a necessary evil for us as product makers, because we can't abdicate and just have someone else be CEO.
zh: 对于我们作为产品制作者来说，这是一种必要的恶，因为我们不能退位而只是让别人成为 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because all of a sudden the priorities get messed up, and teams don't have to listen to us, and we're now feedback, not direction.
zh: 因为突然之间优先级变得混乱，团队不必听我们的，我们现在只是反馈，而不是方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's a failing path.
zh: 所以那是一条失败的道路。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have to be a CEO.
zh: 所以我们必须成为 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then the question is, how do we get to spend the most time doing what we love, and the least time doing what we don't?
zh: 但问题是，我们如何才能花最多时间做我们爱做的事，花最少时间做我们不爱做的事？

speaker: Unknown
en: The bigger principle to me that I started to get to was that, "Oh."
zh: 我开始意识到的更大的原则是，“哦。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "All of management is just how do we get people to do the right thing when we are not in the room?"
zh: 我想，“所有管理只是当我们不在房间里时，我们如何让人们做正确的事？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I was like, "Okay, if I give them a hill to take, and if I make them a CEO."
zh: 所以我想，“好吧，如果我给他们一个山头去攻占，如果我让他们成为 CEO。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Make them a real CEO.
zh: 让他们成为真正的 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, they have operating control degrees of freedom to take that hill however they want, and they can give me a plan, a budget and then do whatever they want within that.
zh: 我的意思是，他们有运营控制度自由，以他们想要的方式攻占那个山头，他们可以给我一个计划、一个预算，然后在那其中做他们想做的任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I found was I didn't have to manage them.
zh: 我发现我不必管理他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they're not coming back to me with questions.
zh: 所以他们不会带着问题回到我这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And certain kinds of people really want to be a CEO.
zh: 某种类型的人真的很想成为 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I also found was it was incredibly motivating for people.
zh: 我还发现这对人们来说是令人难以置信的激励。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's motivating to me.
zh: 这对我来说也是一种激励。

speaker: Unknown
en: I always say I'm a team player as long as I'm running the team.
zh: 我总是说只要我负责团队，我就是团队协作者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So can I hire other people like that?
zh: 所以我可以雇佣其他像我这样的人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like to say I was a frustrated expert witness when I worked for other people, and those are the best people to turn into a CEO.
zh: 而且我喜欢说，我在为别人工作时是一个充满挫败感的专家证人，而这些人是成为 CEO 的最佳人选。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're a little bit of a know it-all.
zh: 他们有点像是一个自以为无所不知的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They think they have the right answers, and we're going to find out if they do.
zh: 他们认为自己拥有正确的答案，而我们将会弄清楚他们是否真的有。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we let them actually be a CEO, and they have all this pent-up demand to actually go and prove that they were right, and that's a beautiful thing.
zh: 所以我们让他们真正成为一名 CEO，他们拥有所有这些被压抑的需求，去实际证明他们是对的，这是一件美好的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I love this principle of make everyone a CEO.
zh: 所以是的，我热爱让每个人都成为 CEO 这一原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're going there in Silicon Valley with this idea of get rid of middle management.
zh: 我们正在硅谷推行这一理念，即摆脱中层管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Brian Armstrong's saying, "Everyone should be an individual contributor.
zh: Brian Armstrong 说，“每个人都应该是一个独立贡献者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone should be managing a lot of other people."
zh: 每个人都应该管理很多其他人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: To me, the best CEO is the best player at the position, and is doing the thing they're great at and they're not wasting their time on management.
zh: 对我来说，最好的 CEO 是那个位置上最好的选手，正在做他们擅长的事情，而不是把时间浪费在管理上。

speaker: Unknown
en: You mentioned this idea of an expert witness, and you also have this nugget of advice of staying close to the metal.
zh: 你提到了专家证人的这个想法，你也有关注底层这个建议的精华。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this general advice.
zh: 我热爱这个一般性建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what you mean there, why that's important for someone in their career.
zh: 谈谈你在那里的意思，为什么这对职业生涯中的人很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: They go together.
zh: 它们是一致的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We start our careers in our 20s very close to the metal.
zh: 我们在 20 多岁时开始职业生涯，非常接近底层。

speaker: Unknown
en: Meaning we are individual contributors.
zh: 意思就是我们是独立贡献者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we are working on getting the primary data, and building the actual product usually.
zh: 我们正在致力于获取主要数据，并且通常正在构建实际的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're in the trenches.
zh: 我们在战壕里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the people in the trenches are usually closest to the data, closest to probably the right answer, but they're also usually furthest from making the decision.
zh: 在战壕里的人通常最接近数据，最接近可能是正确答案的，但他们也通常最远离做决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what I call the expert witness syndrome.
zh: 这就是我所谓的专家证人综合症。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you're called to the stand by the adults, and you plead for what you passionately believe is the right answer.
zh: 因为你是被成年人传唤到法庭的，你为你热切认为的正确答案辩护。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you're excused, and then they make a decision and then you have to deal with the consequences, and disagree and commit.
zh: 然后你被退庭，然后他们做了一个决定，然后你必须处理后果，并异议但执行。

speaker: Unknown
en: What if I'm disagreeing because I'm fucking right, and now I'm committing to clean up your mess because you're wrong?
zh: 如果我提出异议是因为我特么是对的，而现在我正在致力于清理你的烂摊子因为你错了，那会怎样？

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why many of us become founders, because we just are sick of being in that position.
zh: 这就是我们许多人成为创始人的原因，因为我们只是厌倦了处于那个位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're also closer to the metal.
zh: 你也更接近底层。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you go become a CEO, when you become a founder, part of the paradox of it that I believe we have to be careful about is that we stay close to the metal.
zh: 当你去成为一名 CEO，当你成为一名创始人时，我认为我们必须小心其中的一部分悖论是，我们要保持接近底层。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I believe the best product CEOs are in the minutia of the details.
zh: 而且我认为最好的产品 CEO 专注于细节的细枝末节。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the stories about Steve Jobs, I was so impressed when I heard that he insisted on picking out the carpeting in the conference rooms.
zh: 所有关于 Steve Jobs 的故事，当我听到他坚持亲自挑选会议室的地毯时，我印象太深刻了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I tried to have that same mentality of micromanaging the really small pixel level details that matter.
zh: 我试图拥有同样的心态，即微观管理真正重要的微小像素级细节。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I had a great conversation with the founders of Discord, and they came to this conclusion too that they needed to actually be an inverted pyramid.
zh: 我和 Discord 的创始人进行了一次很棒的对话，他们也得出这个结论，他们需要实际上成为一个倒金字塔。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they said, "We realized that we outsource, we delegate the most important product decisions, the UX to our least experienced people, and they are doing this every day.
zh: 他们说，“我们意识到我们外包了，我们将最重要的产品决策，用户体验委托给了我们经验最少的人，而且他们每天都在做这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we decided to turn that upside down and say, 'We as founders need to be kind of the first and last mile for the product, and we have to be the ones who bless.'
zh: 我们决定把这一点颠倒过来，说，‘我们作为创始人需要成为产品的首尾英里，而且我们必须成为祝福的人。’

speaker: Unknown
en: Our best use of our time is making these minute decisions that change the product user experience."
zh: 我们时间的最大用途是做这些改变产品用户体验的微小决策。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so to me, that's being close to the metal.
zh: 所以对我来说，这就是接近底层。

speaker: Unknown
en: Brian Chesky believes in doing things in non-scalable ways, and doing it himself with the team by hand.
zh: Brian Chesky 相信以不可扩展的方式做事，并亲自与团队手工完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so many stories about Jeff Bezos and Zuck of the thing that mattered most.
zh: 关于 Jeff Bezos 和 Zuck 在最重要的事情上有很多故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: They would go and work two days a week deeply with the team on it.
zh: 他们会去每周与团队深入工作两天。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it makes sense.
zh: 这是有道理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you were the best product maker in the company, we want you on the field.
zh: 如果你是公司最好的产品制造者，我们希望你在这个领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want to put you on the ice.
zh: 我们希望把你放在冰面上。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want you to spend your time talking to investors, and managing and scaling.
zh: 我们不希望你花时间与投资者交谈，以及管理和扩张。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, Elon famous for this too.
zh: 是的，Elon 也因此而出名。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have this line in the book along these lines, "Micromanagement is beautiful.
zh: 你在书中有一行话与此类似，“微观管理是美好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should micromanage as long as you can."
zh: 你应该尽可能久地进行微观管理。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe that, and I did that building Zynga to a fault.
zh: 我相信这一点，我在构建 Zynga 时那样做了到了一个错误的地步。

speaker: Unknown
en: We got to a point that I had all the way to 50 employees.
zh: 我们到了一个点，我有 50 名员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had a standup call that would go on for two hours, and I had everyone's name on a spreadsheet, and I had what they were supposed to do yesterday and what they're going to do today.
zh: 我们有一个站会电话会持续两个小时，我把每个人的名字都在电子表格上，我有他们昨天应该做什么和今天要做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I go through name by name and say, "Okay, did you get that done?
zh: 我逐个过一遍名字并说，“好的，你做完那个了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can everyone else verify they got that done?
zh: 其他人都能验证他们做完那个了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, what are you going to do today?"
zh: 好的，你今天要做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I completely micromanaged, but we were much more effective.
zh: 我完全微观管理了，但我们有效率得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I kept micromanaging things as...
zh: 然后我继续事必躬亲地管理各种事情……

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "If you can be in the room, be in the room assuming that you are the best player."
zh: 我会说，“如果你能在这个房间里，那就待在这个房间里，并假设你是最优秀的选手。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I think it's less contrarian saying it now than it was 20 years ago.
zh: 是的，我认为现在这样说比20年前不那么反潮流了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when I was building Zynga, we had to apologize for things like micromanaging, or expecting massive results from our teams.
zh: 但当我建立 Zynga 时，我们不得不为诸如事必躬亲的管理，或期待团队取得巨大成果之类的事情道歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think I'm happy to see that that's becoming accepted as more...
zh: 而且我很高兴看到这正被更多地接受为……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's closer to best practices today, I think.
zh: 我认为这今天更接近于最佳实践。

speaker: Unknown
en: But my point about that management is get people to do the right thing when you're not in the room.
zh: 但我关于那种管理的观点是，让你不在场时人们也能做正确的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first principle is be in the room if you can be, as much as you can be.
zh: 第一条准则是，如果你能在这个房间里，就尽你所能多待在这个房间里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then only delegate when you get to the point that you can't possibly be in all these rooms at the same time.
zh: 然后只有当你到了不可能同时出现在所有这些房间的时候，才进行授权。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then all these management principles are just different strategies to try to get people to do the right thing when you're not there.
zh: 而后所有这些管理原则都只是试图让你不在场时人们也能做正确事的不同策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love these non-scalable ideas that actually scale what you're trying to do as a product maker in the right way, and one of the ones I love is this idea of tech assistance, and this idea of a teaching hospital.
zh: 我喜欢这些实际上作为产品制造者以正确方式扩展你正在做的事情的不可扩展的想法，其中我喜欢的一个是这个技术助理的想法，以及这个教学医院的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you pass the vampire blood of you to other people?
zh: 你如何把你的吸血鬼之血传递给其他人？

speaker: Unknown
en: You have this burning mission and passion around your product.
zh: 你对你的产品有着这种燃烧般的使命感和热情。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you spread that to other people and have them spread in the organization?
zh: 你如何将其传播给其他人并让他们在组织中传播？

speaker: Unknown
en: The first thing is put them in the room with you.
zh: 第一件事是让他们和你待在同一个房间里。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the idea of this teaching hospital was, "Okay, I'm just going to put as many people as I can in the room while I'm doing these product management meetings, and have my passion, and ideas, and approach spread to all of them too."
zh: 所以这个教学医院的想法是，“好吧，在进行这些产品管理会议时，我要把尽可能多的人放在房间里，让我的热情、想法和方法也传播给他们所有人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that worked.
zh: 而且这奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then grab someone from the ranks and make them your tech assistant.
zh: 然后从队伍中抓一个人让他做你的技术助理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Have them just follow you around for six months, or 12 months.
zh: 让他们只是跟随你六个月，或12个月。

speaker: Unknown
en: And make it the mini me.
zh: 并让他成为迷你版的你。

speaker: Unknown
en: The person who is you in your 20s who was the expert witness, a little bit of a know it all.
zh: 那个是你20岁时的样子的人，他是专家证人，有点像个万事通。

speaker: Unknown
en: And start having them follow you around to pick up, learn, and absorb everything you do.
zh: 并开始让他们跟随你，以便学习、理解并吸收你所做的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: And give them projects to test them.
zh: 并给他们项目来测试他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then hopefully, you've created a mini me that you can now put in in a much bigger role somewhere else.
zh: 然后有希望的是，你已经创造了一个迷你版的你，你现在可以把他放在别处一个更大的角色中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, Andy Jassy at Amazon, everyone on the C staff used to be someone who'd been the tech assistant to Bezos.
zh: 事实上，亚马逊的 Andy Jassy，C层员工中的每一个人过去都曾是贝索斯的技术助理。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that idea actually scales, and you get it for free.
zh: 所以这个想法实际上确实可以扩展，而且你是免费得到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this phrase of transferring your vampire blood.
zh: 我喜欢这个转移你的吸血鬼之血的说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe one last nugget from the book along these lines is you have this line of just the number one job of a CEO is to be right.
zh: 也许书里沿着这条线的最后一个金点是，你有这一行，CEO 的头号工作就是正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that.
zh: 谈谈那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, that's another one that I stole from Bezos.
zh: 嗯，那又一条是我从贝索斯那里偷来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if I get to pick one thing that you do as CEO, I'm going to pick that you're right.
zh: 但如果我必须选一件你作为 CEO 做的事情，我会选你是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if you don't operate the ship or the factory that well, I'd rather that you picked the right product, the right strategy than your phenomenal execution, or inspiring people or managing.
zh: 即使你没有很好地掌管这艘船或工厂，我宁愿你选择了正确的产品、正确的策略，而不是你出色的执行力，或鼓舞人心或管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because being in the right body of water matters more than the right boat, and a great boat in a dead lake bed isn't going anywhere.
zh: 因为处于正确的水域比正确的船更重要，而在干涸的湖床中的一艘好船哪儿也去不了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I look for that in people, like, "Are they right?"
zh: 所以我在人们身上寻找这一点，比如，“他们是正确的吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the best resume, I could see that you were right about something.
zh: 那是最好的简历，我可以看到你关于某件事是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I look for that in the team, and it's less do I like your style, or approach, or your personality fit?
zh: 而且我在团队中寻找这一点，它更少是我喜欢你的风格、方法或你的个性合拍吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "I'll take misfits who are right."
zh: 我会说，“我会接受那些不合群但正确的人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "I want those really smart expert witnesses who are intellectually honest."
zh: 我会说，“我想要那些真正聪明的专家证人，他们在智力上是诚实的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the more people you have around you who are right, they're putting balls in the net.
zh: 你周围有越多正确的人，他们就在把球踢进网里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to maybe end with a question about your kids and parenting.
zh: 我想也许最后问一个关于你的孩子和育儿的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I talked to a bunch of people that know you well in preparation for this conversation, and every single person commented on just how wonderful a dad you are, and how much time you put into that part of your life.
zh: 为了这次对话，我和一群很了解你的人谈过，每一个人都评论说你是一个多么棒的父亲，以及你在你生活的那部分投入了多少时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a question I've been trying to ask folks that are parents and great parents.
zh: 我一直试图问那些身为父母且很棒的父母的一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: In this AI era, and there's a lot of stuff I want to ask about, but let me start here.
zh: 在这个 AI 时代，有很多我想问的事情，但让我从这里开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just what's something that you are teaching them or helping them to develop that you think is going to be important to them in this crazy world we're entering of AI?
zh: 就是你正在教给他们什么或帮助他们发展什么，你认为这在我们要进入的这个疯狂的 AI 世界中对他们来说是重要的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for asking about this.
zh: 谢谢你问这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think my greatest role, and job, and service to the world is trying to grow good humans.
zh: 我认为我最大的角色、工作和服务世界是试图培养优秀的人类。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I really, really put a lot into it and take it seriously, and I get so much out of it.
zh: 所以我真的真的投入很多，认真对待它，并从中获得很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to say, "I'm growing my best friends."
zh: 我喜欢说，“我正在培养我最好的朋友。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so far, it's true.
zh: 到目前为止，这是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a couple of principles that have come in.
zh: 所以这里有几个原则进来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Philosophies that have been important to me as a parent that I've just seen...
zh: 作为一个父母，对我来说很重要的哲学，我只是看到……

speaker: Unknown
en: I do them naturally, and I see that they work over time.
zh: 我自然地这样做，而且我发现它们随着时间的推移是有效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first one for me is I have five kids, they're... Every one of them is just such a different version of a human.
zh: 对我来说第一个原则是我有五个孩子，他们……每一个都是人类如此不同的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I have a special needs son who's fairly extreme special needs.
zh: 我有一个有特殊需要的儿子，他的特殊需要相当极端。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our new one-year-old baby has a gene mutation, and she actually may also end up being very neurodivergent.
zh: 我们新的一岁宝宝有一个基因突变，她实际上最终也可能非常神经多样性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I used to have this wide range.
zh: 所以我曾经有这个广泛的范围。

speaker: Unknown
en: My 15-year-old girls are...
zh: 我 15 岁的女孩们是……

speaker: Unknown
en: They're twins, and they're completely divergent from each other.
zh: 她们是双胞胎，而且她们彼此完全发散。

speaker: Unknown
en: This first principle that I've found is meeting them where they are.
zh: 我发现的第一个原则是在他们所在的地方与他们相遇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's with kids I find we can talk down to them, we can talk to them as kids, or, we can treat them like adults and neither of those are right.
zh: 而且对于孩子，我发现我们可以对他们居高临下地说话，我们可以像对孩子一样对他们说话，或者，我们可以像对待成人一样对待他们，这两者都不对。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, "We got to engage with them at the altitude that they're at.
zh: 这就像是，“我们必须在他们所处的那个高度与他们互动。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as human to human, and find the way to do that."
zh: 但是作为人对人，并找到那样做的方法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then once we do that and meet them where they are, I think we can bring them to surprisingly sophisticated places that may be way beyond where they're supposed to be in that moment.
zh: 然后一旦我们那样做并在他们所在的地方与他们相遇，我认为我们可以把他们带到令人惊讶的复杂地方，这些地方可能远远超出了他们在那一刻应该在的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like my twins, I started playing with math with them when they were really, really little.
zh: 像我的双胞胎，我在她们非常、非常小的时候就开始和她们玩数学。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then during the pandemic, I taught daddy math because I saw that their school was not equipped to do online homeschooling.
zh: 然后在疫情期间，我教了爸爸数学，因为我看到他们的学校没有能力进行在线家庭学校教育。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I taught them and their friends daddy math.
zh: 所以我教了她们和她们的朋友爸爸数学。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just tried to teach them a math brain, and I didn't know where they were supposed to be, they were in fifth grade.
zh: 我只是试图教给她们一个数学大脑，我不知道她们应该在什么地方，她们在五年级。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I just started for them and their friends just trying to teach them how to approach math in a way that's fun and curious.
zh: 但我只是为她们和她们的朋友开始，试图教她们如何以一种有趣和好奇的方式来接近数学。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we found out later I'd taught them all the way through eighth grade math.
zh: 然后我们后来发现，我教完了她们直到八年级的数学。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I didn't know it, and they didn't know it and that was cool.
zh: 我不知道，她们也不知道，这很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I'd say in the... How does this apply in the age of AI and where we are?
zh: 然后我想说在……这如何应用在 AI 时代和我们所在的地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like we're at the end of this 100-year cycle of mass-produced education, which was get the most knowledge in, get everyone's averages up to the best place we can, educate 20 million graduates a year or whatever number.
zh: 我感觉我们处于这个 100 年大规模生产教育周期的末端，这是要把最多的知识灌输进去，把每个人的平均水准提高到我们能做到的最好位置，每年教育 2000 万毕业生或随便什么数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was factory produced, and it was for a certain kind of work.
zh: 但这是工厂生产的，它是为了某种特定的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: First factories, then knowledge workers.
zh: 首先是工厂，然后是知识工作者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Knowledge working is going away, it's changing.
zh: 知识工作正在消失，它正在改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we're still teaching knowledge education, and I feel badly about it with my kids.
zh: 但我们仍然在教授知识教育，我为此对我的孩子感到糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I've tried to do with my older kids is teach them to have critical thinking more than... And I say, "I don't care if you go to college," which is a little hard for them because they're achievers.
zh: 我试着对我大一点的孩子做的是教他们拥有批判性思维，而不是……我说，“我不在乎你们是否上大学”，这对他们来说有点困难，因为他们都是成就者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And like, "I don't care if you go to college.
zh: 就像是，“我不在乎你们是否上大学。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I care about is that you develop critical thinking, and you find a way to be useful to people in the world."
zh: 我在乎的是你们发展批判性思维，并且你们找到一种在世界上的方式来对人们有用。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And my one daughter, Carmen, she's also neurodivergent.
zh: 我的一个女儿，Carmen，她也是神经多样性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: She has ADHD and dyslexia, and she's created a brand of sweatshirts called Comfy Fancy.
zh: 她有 ADHD 和阅读障碍，她创建了一个叫 Comfy Fancy 的卫衣品牌。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she also has created a group for neurodivergent middle school kids called Neurosparkly.
zh: 她还为神经多样性的中学生创建了一个叫 Neurosparkly 的团体。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's taking this thing which should be a problem and a deficit, and it's making it a way that she can connect and help a lot of other kids.
zh: 这是把本应该是一个问题和缺陷的东西，变成了一种她可以连接和帮助很多其他孩子的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm proud to see that.
zh: 所以我很自豪看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: The dissonance that I feel in this moment as a parent is that my kids are in this educational system that is trying to prep them to get into a good college and to be a knowledge worker, and I know that game is over.
zh: 作为父母，我在这一刻感到的不和谐是，我的孩子处于这个试图为他们准备进入好大学并成为知识工作者的教育体系中，而我知道那个游戏已经结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what do I want my kids doing?
zh: 那么我想要我的孩子做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I try to teach them to... Look at the meta of what's going on.
zh: 我试着教他们……看看正在发生事情的元层面。

speaker: Unknown
en: When an adult is trying to convince you of something.
zh: 当一个成年人试图说服你某事时。

speaker: Unknown
en: When a teacher has a point of view that you don't agree with, try to understand why.
zh: 当老师有一个你不同意观点时，试着理解为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's their agenda?
zh: 他们的议程是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's their lived experience, and how has that impacted it?
zh: 他们的生活经历是什么，这如何影响了它？

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm trying to teach them to ask better questions, not know more answers.
zh: 所以我试着教他们提出更好的问题，而不是知道更多的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'd say in terms of how that spills into their online use and what I try to promote and discourage, I try to promote how can you be generative in what you're doing and not consumptive?
zh: 我想说在如何渗透到他们的在线使用方面，以及我试图鼓励和劝阻什么方面，我试图鼓励你如何在你所做的事情中具有生成性，而不是消费性？

speaker: Unknown
en: So what can you do online or offline that is generative, that is actually putting something new out in the world, because that's how you're going to get something back?
zh: 那么你可以在网上或线下做什么具有生成性的事情，实际上是向世界推出了新的东西，因为那是你将得到回报的方式？

speaker: Unknown
en: And not consuming, not being a passive consumer of content and experiences.
zh: 而不是消费，不是成为内容和体验的被动消费者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes it works, and I see them doing that, and then sometimes it doesn't.
zh: 有时它奏效了，我看到她们那样做，然后有时它没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's how I'm trying to navigate this.
zh: 但这就是我试图驾驭这个的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also tried to make it until they were 16 with no phones, no smartphones.
zh: 我也试图坚持到她们 16 岁没有手机，没有智能手机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I got to 14, I got them flip phones.
zh: 我坚持到了 14 岁，给了她们翻盖手机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they had internships at Niantic working on Pokemon Go.
zh: 而且他们在 Niantic 实习，参与制作 Pokémon Go。

speaker: Unknown
en: They built a first time user experience.
zh: 他们构建了一个首次用户体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really good that they were, I think, used.
zh: 我觉得他们被（录用）是很好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they had to get smartphones for that, and they still have them.
zh: 但为此他们得配备智能手机，而且他们现在还有。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tried to make it, and I didn't.
zh: 我试着去（做成）那样，但我没做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh man, that's hilarious.
zh: 噢天哪，太逗了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, and also, I'll tell you one more thing.
zh: 噢，还有，我要再告诉你一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: For my older girls, I keep a running Google doc of life philosophies.
zh: 对于我的大女儿们，我维护了一个持续更新的生活哲学 Google 文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: And anything I say to them as a quote I say over and over, I'm like, "Okay, I'm going to actually write that up with stories, and I keep it in this document for them and who knows if they'll use it."
zh: 任何我反复对她们说的名言，我都会说，“好吧，我要把它结合故事写下来，我保存在这个文档里给她们，天晓得她们会不会用。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But they do start to repeat some of them, so I know they read it.
zh: 但她们确实开始重复其中的一些了，所以我知道她们读了。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is nothing's personal.
zh: 一条是什么都别针对个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't ever take anything personal.
zh: 永远别把任何事情针对个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you assume nothing's personal, you're probably right 19 out of 20 times, and the 20th time you probably handled it right because of that.
zh: 如果你假设没有任何事是针对个人的，你 20 次里有 19 次可能是对的，而第 20 次你可能也因此处理得当。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's one.
zh: 所以这是其中一条。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't be a victim.
zh: 还有，别做一个受害者。

speaker: Unknown
en: The world doesn't happen to you, it's happening around you.
zh: 世界不是发生在你身上的，它是围绕你发生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're defined how we react to the world, not by the events that happen to us.
zh: 我们通过如何对世界做出反应来定义，而不是通过发生在我们身上的事件。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm trying to give them these.
zh: 所以我试图给她们这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're teenagers, or the older two, and I'm in it and learning.
zh: 她们是青少年，或者说大的两个是，我也身在其中并在学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've got two littles that... A one-year-old and four-year-old who sleep with me every night and-
zh: 我还有两个小的……一个一岁和一个四岁的，每晚都跟我睡，而且——

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're busy.
zh: 你很忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it's clearly what your next book is going to be is these life philosophies.
zh: 我感觉很明显，你的下一本书将会是这些生活哲学。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like these are useful for people, for humans.
zh: 我感觉这些对人们、对人类都有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: For adults, I mean.
zh: 我是说对成年人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wanted to write a book on parenting saying, "Everything I know about parenting, I learned from my dog Zynga."
zh: 我想写一本关于育儿的书，说“我所知关于育儿的一切，都是从我的狗 Zynga 那里学来的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I had her before I had kids, and she was like a person.
zh: 因为我有孩子之前就有她，而且她像个人一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I gave her clear boundaries, and freedom and love in between, and she believed that she was a person.
zh: 我给她清晰的界限，以及中间的自由和爱，而且她相信她是一个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else you wanted to share before we wrap up?
zh: 在结束之前你还有什么想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: The thing that I've gotten, that's resonated most in writing in my book of life for the last 30 years.
zh: 这件事我得到了，就是在过去 30 年写我的人生之书中产生共鸣最多的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It took me a long, long time.
zh: 我花了很长很长的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: It took me until I started Zynga at 41 to finally identify and articulate my why.
zh: 直到我在 41 岁创办 Zynga 时，才终于确定并阐明了我的“为什么”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think for all of us, it's so important to get there.
zh: 我认为对我们所有人来说，到达那里至关重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we might do for a long time before we actually can say the why.
zh: 我们可能要做很长一段时间，才能真正说出那个“为什么”。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I figured out that mine, what I talk about in the book, is I want to create an internet treasure.
zh: 但我发现我的，正如我在书中谈论的那样，是我想创造一个互联网宝藏。

speaker: Unknown
en: When people say, "Mark, how do I know that you're going to work hard on this next thing, or you're post-economic, or you're a bored, rich guy or whatever?"
zh: 当人们说，“Mark，我怎么知道你会在下一件事上努力工作，或者你已经后经济时代了，或者你是个无聊的富人或者其他什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Well, I haven't done my thing yet."
zh: 我会说，“嗯，我还没做成我的事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, "What does our soul need to do before we die, or at least to be in integrity with it?
zh: 就像，“我们的灵魂在死前需要做什么，或者至少为了保持它的完整性？”

speaker: Unknown
en: What do I need to be working on and know that I gave everything I had against it?"
zh: 我需要致力于什么，并知道我为此倾尽所有？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's to build an internet treasure, which is a service we can't remember life before or imagine life without.
zh: 那就是建立一个互联网宝藏，也就是一种我们在之前无法记住生活、或之后无法想象生活的服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I believe as product makers, that that's the greatest ambition and the greatest thing that we can offer the world.
zh: 我相信作为产品制造者，那是我们可以提供给世界的最大雄心和最伟大的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my friend Bing Gordon says, "One day, those treasures will be in the Smithsonian."
zh: 我的朋友 Bing Gordon 说，“总有一天，那些宝藏会进入史密森尼学会。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think he's right.
zh: 我觉得他是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it is the greatest opportunity that we have as product makers in this era, is to build those digital skyscrapers that the next generation can't believe anyone ever lived without.
zh: 我认为这是我们在这个时代作为产品制造者拥有的最大机会，就是建造那些下一代人无法相信曾经没有它们就能生活的数字摩天大楼。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's my ambition, and why I'm still rubbing sticks together and... Yeah, it is.
zh: 那是我的雄心，也是为什么我还在钻木取火而且……是的，正是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And writing books.
zh: 还有写书。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of that, to help people build their own internet treasure, your book is coming out very soon, I think around the time this episode comes out.
zh: 说到这个，为了帮助人们建立他们自己的互联网宝藏，你的书很快就要出版了，我想大概就在这一集播出的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell people where to find it.
zh: 告诉人们哪里能找到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else you want to share about the book?
zh: 关于这本书你还有什么想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's right here.
zh: 就在这儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, you have a copy.
zh: 嗯，你有副本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just got a copy.
zh: 我刚拿到副本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two copies somehow.
zh: 不知怎么有两个副本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two.
zh: 两本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you're important.
zh: 因为你很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: We hope you read it twice.
zh: 我们希望你能读两遍。

speaker: Lenny
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Give it out to a friend.
zh: 送一本给朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would just say that Life at the Speed of Play is my...
zh: 我只想说，《玩速人生》（Life at the Speed of Play）是我的……

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm trying to share my playbook and philosophies, and I'm hopeful that somebody will steal some of my ideas and take it further.
zh: 我正在试图分享我的手册和理念，我希望有人能窃取我的一些想法并将其发扬光大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're all in a conversation, and I love this podcast and you hosting this cocktail party.
zh: 我们都在进行一场对话，我很喜欢这个播客和你主持的这场鸡尾酒会。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're all trying to move the philosophies and the craft of product making ahead, and we're all learning as we go.
zh: 我们都在试图推动产品制作的哲学和技艺向前发展，我们都在沿途学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I hope that my book can be something referenceable, and I hope it's easy and fun to read, because I have pain reading a lot of books.
zh: 我希望我的书能成为可参考的东西，我希望它读起来轻松有趣，因为我读很多书时感到痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is actually very easy to read.
zh: 实际上它很容易读。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very not that long, and it's very bite-sized, so great job with that.
zh: 它不是很长，而且是非常小块的，所以在这方面做得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, good.
zh: 噢，好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mark, thank you so much for being here.
zh: Mark，非常感谢你来这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks for sharing all this wisdom with us.
zh: 谢谢你与我们分享所有这些智慧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is fun.
zh: 这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is actually, I've done some podcasts about the book, and I didn't want to talk about the content of the book on most of the podcast, and on this one, I did.
zh: 实际上，我做了一些关于这本书的播客，在大多数播客中我不想谈论这本书的内容，但在这一期里，我谈了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm trying to only go into places that I want to talk about, which I tried to do in the book too.
zh: 我试图只去那些我想谈论的地方，我在书中也试图这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: There we go.
zh: 就这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: So thank you for inspiring me to want to talk about product.
zh: 所以谢谢你激励我想谈论产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: My pleasure.
zh: 我的荣幸。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks, Mark, bye everyone.
zh: 谢谢，Mark，再见各位。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢您的收听。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you found this valuable, you can subscribe to the show on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app.
zh: 如果您觉得有价值，您可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或您喜欢的播客应用上订阅本节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们评分或留下评论，这真的能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com.
zh: 您可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到所有过往剧集或了解有关该节目的更多信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: See you in the next episode.
zh: 下一集见。
